REGULATORY_COMPLIANCE.description: What must be true about compliance posture before regulated activities begin

blocks.condition: Any legally required permits, licenses, or registrations must be in place before regulated activity begins
examples: financial services license must be secured before offering regulated products
data broker registration must be complete before data resale begins
healthcare certification must be obtained before processing PHI

blocks.condition: Policies, processes, and technical controls must be operational before compliance is claimed or audited
examples: access control policies must be live before SOC 2 audit begins
data encryption must be implemented before claiming HIPAA compliance
consent management must be deployed before GDPR audit

blocks.condition: A current, accurate privacy policy must be live before customer data is collected
examples: privacy policy must be published before a website collects contact details
data processing terms must be available before a B2B product goes live
cookie consent policy must be implemented before tracking is activated

blocks.condition: Staff must have completed required training before they handle sensitive data or regulated processes
examples: GDPR training must be completed before staff access customer data
anti-bribery training must be done before employees work with third parties in high-risk markets
security awareness training must be complete before remote access is granted

blocks.condition: Systems for capturing and retaining evidence of compliance must be running before regulated activities begin
examples: transaction logging must be live before financial activity is processed
communication archiving must be active before regulated correspondence begins
access logs must be captured before sensitive data is accessible to staff

ACQUISITION_INTEGRATION.description: What must be true before an acquisition or integration can succeed

blocks.condition: A thorough investigation of the target must be finished before a definitive acquisition agreement is signed
examples: financial due diligence must clear before a binding offer is made
legal and IP review must be complete before deal closes
technical and product due diligence must be done before integration planning begins

blocks.condition: A clear plan for combining the businesses must exist before close so Day 1 is stable
examples: Day 1 communications plan must be ready before deal closes
system integration roadmap must be agreed before close
org structure for the combined entity must be decided before announcement

blocks.condition: The cultural compatibility of the two organizations must be understood before integration design is finalized
examples: culture gap analysis must be done before team integration planning
values alignment must be assessed before merged leadership team is announced
people risks must be identified before retention plans are built

blocks.condition: Plans to retain key people through the transition must be finalized before deal close
examples: key employee retention packages must be agreed before close
founders must have clear post-close roles before signing
critical technical staff must have retention agreements before product integration begins

blocks.condition: All required government or regulatory approvals must be in place before a transaction can close
examples: antitrust clearance must be obtained before deal close
foreign investment approval must be secured before international acquisition completes
sector-specific regulatory approval must be granted before close

CUSTOMER_HEALTH.description: What must be true about a customer relationship before it can be managed proactively

blocks.condition: A clear plan linking the customer's goals to product outcomes must exist before the relationship can be managed
examples: 90-day success plan must be written during onboarding
renewal success metrics must be agreed before the first QBR
expansion targets must be documented before an account is designated for growth

blocks.condition: Sufficient engagement and usage signals must be captured before account health can be accurately assessed
examples: product usage data must be flowing before health score is meaningful
support ticket data must be available before risk is assessed
QBR completion rate must be tracked before engagement is evaluated

blocks.condition: The full set of contacts and their roles at the customer must be documented before relationship risk can be managed
examples: champion must be identified before an account is considered stable
economic buyer contact must be held before renewal begins
all relevant stakeholders must be mapped before a QBR is planned

blocks.condition: A senior relationship at the customer must be established before strategic expansion is possible
examples: exec sponsor must be identified before a multi-year renewal is pursued
C-level relationship must exist before an enterprise expansion motion begins
executive alignment call must happen before a cross-sell conversation is appropriate

blocks.condition: Open customer concerns, bugs, or commitments must be addressed before the relationship can progress positively
examples: blocking product issue must be resolved before renewal conversation begins
outstanding support commitments must be cleared before a QBR
contractual obligations must be met before expansion is proposed

LAUNCH_READINESS.description: What must be true before a business initiative, product, or market can launch

blocks.condition: Sales, marketing, product, and CS must be coordinated on the launch plan before go-live
examples: all functions must be briefed before a product launch
launch messaging must be consistent across all channels before announcement
sales enablement must be complete before reps are expected to sell a new product

blocks.condition: All defined pre-launch requirements must be satisfied before the launch proceeds
examples: all P0 bugs must be resolved before GA
minimum customer count for a logo must be secured before an announcement
required certifications must be obtained before a regulated product launches

blocks.condition: The support and success teams must be prepared to handle customer questions before launch
examples: support team must be trained on new product before launch
common objections must be addressed in a CS FAQ before go-live
escalation paths must be communicated before new customer volume begins

blocks.condition: All external-facing materials for the launch must be approved before they are delivered to customers or prospects
examples: launch email must be approved before it is sent
press release must be cleared by legal before it is distributed
in-product launch announcement must be reviewed before it goes live

blocks.condition: A way to reverse or pause a launch must exist before going live, in case of unexpected issues
examples: feature flag must be in place before a product launch
revert plan must be documented before a pricing change goes live
communication plan for a pause must be ready before launch

UMG_BUSINESS_NEEDS.description: Prerequisites for deploying UMG in commercial and organizational contexts

blocks.condition: The constraints that define what an AI business agent is and is not permitted to do must be written before deployment
examples: discount authority rules must be in governance before a sales AI agent is deployed
escalation triggers must be defined before a support AI is activated
communication policy must be encoded before an outbound AI agent runs

blocks.condition: The full sleeve — stacks, blocks, NeoBlocks — for the business agent must be architected before compilation
examples: sales agent sleeve must be complete before it is compiled for deployment
customer success agent blocks must be authored before the Envoy is activated
BI agent sleeve must cover the required analytical scope before use

blocks.condition: Business stakeholders must sign off on the governance rules before an AI agent operates under them commercially
examples: legal and compliance must approve AI communication policy before deployment
sales leadership must approve discount authority limits in governance before agent launches
finance must review pricing constraint blocks before a commercial AI is activated

blocks.condition: The success metrics for the AI agent deployment must be agreed before it goes live
examples: target metrics for AI-assisted deals must be set before a sales agent is deployed
support resolution rate targets must be agreed before an AI support agent is activated
BI agent accuracy benchmarks must be defined before it produces board-level outputs

blocks.condition: The conditions under which the AI agent hands off to a human must be defined and tested before deployment
examples: escalation triggers from AI to human AE must be tested before a sales agent goes live
support escalation criteria must be defined before an AI agent handles customer tickets
compliance exception handling must be routed to human review before a regulated AI agent is deployed

title: need.coding.library.v1.0.0
reason: unsupported role need preserved in legacy only

legacySource.description: Coding-domain MOLT Need blocks. Need answers: 'What must be true first?' Each block describes a software engineering prerequisite, enabling condition, or required state that must exist before the coding Aim can be achieved.
legacySource.usage: Select the Need block that describes what must already be in place before the coding Aim can succeed. If the Need is absent, the Aim cannot be reached regardless of effort.

ENVIRONMENT_SETUP.description: What must be true about the development environment before work can begin

blocks.condition: The required language runtime or interpreter must be present and at the correct version before code can run
examples: Node.js must be installed at the required version before npm scripts run
Python must be present in the virtual environment before the app starts
the JVM must be installed before a Java build runs

blocks.condition: All packages listed in the project manifest must be installed before code can import them
examples: npm install must complete before a Node app starts
pip install -r requirements.txt must succeed before running tests
cargo build must resolve all crates before compilation

blocks.condition: All required runtime configuration values must be present in the environment before the application starts
examples: DATABASE_URL must be set before the app can connect to a database
API_SECRET must be injected before auth middleware works
PORT must be defined before the server binds

blocks.condition: Any local dependencies the application requires — databases, message brokers, mock services — must be up before the app starts
examples: a local Postgres instance must be running before the ORM connects
a local Redis must be up before the session store initialises
a mock API server must be running before integration tests use it

blocks.need: Repository cloned and branch checked out
blocks.condition: The source code must be available locally on the correct branch before any development work begins
examples: the repo must be cloned before running the project locally
the feature branch must be checked out before implementing a ticket
the correct tag must be checked out before a hotfix is applied

CODE_AVAILABILITY.description: What must be true about the codebase before it can be worked on

blocks.condition: The existing codebase must compile or type-check cleanly before new work is layered on top
examples: the TypeScript project must compile before new code is added
the Rust project must build before a new module is introduced
the existing test suite must be in a passing state before a refactor begins

blocks.condition: All existing tests must be green before new changes are introduced, so regressions can be detected
examples: the full test suite must pass before a PR is opened
no failing tests must exist before a refactor begins
the regression suite must be green before a release is cut

blocks.condition: The specific file, function, or module to be changed must be located before work begins
examples: the function with the bug must be found before a fix is written
the module to be refactored must be identified before restructuring begins
the endpoint to be optimised must be located before profiling

blocks.condition: The developer must understand how the target code fits into the larger system before making changes
examples: the dependencies of a module must be understood before it is extracted
the callers of a function must be identified before its signature is changed
the data flow through a component must be understood before state is modified

blocks.condition: The git history must be available so regressions can be traced and changes can be understood in context
examples: git log must be available to bisect a regression
blame history must be accessible to understand why a line was written
the full commit history must be present before an ADR references prior decisions

SPECIFICATION_CLARITY.description: What must be true about requirements before implementation begins

blocks.condition: The conditions that determine whether an implementation is correct must be stated before coding begins
examples: user story acceptance criteria must be written before a feature is implemented
the expected return values of a function must be specified before it is written
the API contract must be agreed before the handler is implemented

blocks.condition: What is in and out of scope for the current task must be clear before work starts
examples: the boundary of a refactor must be agreed before touching code
which edge cases are in scope must be clear before writing tests
whether backwards compatibility is required must be decided before changing an API

blocks.condition: The API, function signature, or data contract must be specified before implementation begins
examples: the REST endpoint shape must be agreed before the handler is written
the function signature must be decided before the body is implemented
the event schema must be finalised before the producer and consumer are coded

blocks.condition: Known boundary conditions and unexpected inputs must be listed before implementation begins so they are addressed
examples: null input handling must be specified before the function is written
empty array behaviour must be defined before a list-processing function is coded
overflow handling must be decided before arithmetic code is written

blocks.condition: How the code should behave when things go wrong must be defined before happy-path implementation begins
examples: what to return on a database connection failure must be specified before a repository is written
whether to throw or return an error must be decided before a parser is coded
which HTTP status codes map to which failure modes must be agreed before a controller is implemented

TEST_INFRASTRUCTURE.description: What must be true before tests can be written or run

blocks.condition: The testing library and runner must be present and configured before tests can be authored or executed
examples: Jest must be installed and configured before unit tests are written
pytest must be present before Python tests run
the test runner must be wired into the CI pipeline before tests are required to pass

blocks.condition: Tests must run in an environment that does not affect or depend on production state
examples: tests must use a separate test database before they mutate data
tests must mock external APIs before they run in CI without network access
test fixtures must be isolated before tests run in parallel safely

blocks.condition: Test doubles for external dependencies must exist before tests that rely on them can be written
examples: a mock for the payment gateway must be created before checkout tests can run
database fixtures must be seeded before integration tests can run
a fake file system must be set up before file-handling tests can be written

blocks.condition: For TDD and bug fixes, a test that demonstrates the current failure must exist before the fix or feature is implemented
examples: a failing test that reproduces the bug must be written before the patch
a failing acceptance test must exist before the feature is built
a failing property test must be present before the algorithm is corrected

blocks.condition: The current test coverage level must be measured and recorded before it is used as a comparison point
examples: current coverage percentage must be recorded before a coverage improvement sprint
a coverage report must exist before CI enforces a minimum threshold
coverage baseline must be stored before a refactor claims it did not reduce coverage

BUILD_PIPELINE.description: What must be true about the build system before code can be compiled or packaged

blocks.condition: The build system must be set up and producing a correct output before development relies on it
examples: Webpack config must be correct before the bundle is trusted
the Makefile must be present and correct before make commands are used
the Gradle build must be configured before the project compiles

blocks.condition: The project must build without warnings or errors before the output is deployed or used
examples: TypeScript must compile with zero errors before a release is cut
the Docker image must build successfully before it is pushed to a registry
the binary must link without warnings before it is used in a test

blocks.condition: The same source code must always produce the same artifact regardless of when or where it is built
examples: lockfile must be committed before reproducible installs are guaranteed
base Docker image must be pinned before builds are reproducible
build timestamps must be excluded before binary reproducibility is verified

blocks.condition: For performance-sensitive workflows, the cache must be populated before build times are acceptable
examples: Docker layer cache must be primed before CI build times are reasonable
npm cache must be populated before a fast install is expected
Gradle build cache must be warm before incremental builds are fast

blocks.condition: The registry where build outputs are pushed must be reachable before a build can publish its artifact
examples: Docker Hub must be reachable before an image is pushed
npm registry must be accessible before a package is published
the internal artifact repository must be reachable before a CI deploy pushes a build

DEBUGGING_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before a bug can be diagnosed and fixed

blocks.condition: The failure must be triggerable reliably before a fix can be written
examples: a minimal reproduction case must exist before debugging begins
the exact input that causes the crash must be known before tracing
the sequence of steps that triggers the bug must be documented before fixing

blocks.condition: Observable evidence of the failure must be present before root cause analysis begins
examples: the stack trace must be available before the crash is diagnosed
the error log must be captured before the failure location is determined
the failing request payload must be recorded before the input is examined

blocks.condition: The build must include debug information before a debugger can be attached or symbols resolved
examples: the binary must be built with debug symbols before gdb can show line numbers
source maps must be generated before a browser debugger shows TypeScript lines
the app must be running in debug mode before a breakpoint can be set

blocks.condition: Which users, data, or systems are affected must be established before the severity and fix priority are determined
examples: the number of affected requests must be known before escalation level is set
the affected data range must be understood before a remediation plan is written
the affected services must be identified before the fix is scoped

blocks.condition: A test that captures the bug must exist before the fix is applied, so it can be verified as resolved and guarded against return
examples: a failing unit test must be written before the bug is patched
a failing integration test must exist before the service interaction is fixed
a property test that exposes the edge case must be authored before the algorithm is corrected

DEPENDENCY_STATE.description: What must be true about third-party code before it can be used

blocks.condition: The version of a dependency being used must be compatible with the project's runtime and other packages
examples: peer dependency requirements must be met before a package is installed
the library version must support the Node.js version in use
the package must be compatible with the framework version before it is added

blocks.condition: A dependency must be free of known critical security vulnerabilities before it is used in production
examples: a CVE audit must pass before a package is added to a production build
a vulnerable transitive dependency must be resolved before a release is cut
npm audit must report no critical issues before deployment

blocks.condition: The licence of a dependency must be compatible with the project's distribution model before it is added
examples: a GPL licence must be confirmed as acceptable before adding it to a proprietary project
all licences must be reviewed before open-sourcing an internal tool
licence approval must be obtained before a package is added to a commercial product

blocks.condition: The dependency lockfile must be checked into version control before builds are reproducible across environments
examples: package-lock.json must be committed before CI installs match local
Cargo.lock must be in the repo before build outputs are consistent
poetry.lock must be committed before the Python environment is reproducible

blocks.condition: The interface and behaviour of the dependency must be understood before it is used, to avoid misuse or surprise
examples: the library's error throwing behaviour must be understood before it is wrapped
pagination semantics of an SDK must be read before a list API is called
the rate limits of a client library must be known before it is used in a high-volume context

SCHEMA_DATA_MODEL.description: What must be true about data structures before code operates on them

blocks.condition: The data structure must be specified and not subject to imminent change before code is written against it
examples: the database schema must be agreed before ORM models are written
the API response schema must be finalised before a client is coded
the event format must be stable before consumer code is written

blocks.condition: All pending database migrations must be run before the application code that depends on the new schema is executed
examples: the column must be added before the ORM field can be used
the table rename migration must be applied before queries using the new name are run
the index must be created before the query that relies on it is expected to be fast

blocks.condition: Representative data must exist in the test database before tests that query or modify it can run
examples: user fixtures must be seeded before authentication tests run
product records must exist before cart tests can add items
baseline data must be present before a migration test verifies transformation

blocks.condition: The types and nullability of all fields must be decided before code that reads or writes them is authored
examples: whether a timestamp is nullable must be agreed before the ORM field is defined
whether an ID is a string or integer must be decided before it is used in a route
the precision of a monetary amount must be specified before arithmetic on it is implemented

blocks.condition: The rules that determine whether input data is acceptable must be defined before the validation layer is coded
examples: email format requirements must be specified before a validator is written
password strength rules must be agreed before a password input is validated
required vs. optional fields must be listed before a schema validator is configured

ACCESS_AND_PERMISSIONS.description: What must be true about access control before protected resources can be used

blocks.condition: Credentials for external services must exist and be injected before any code that calls those services can run
examples: an API key for a mapping service must be issued before the integration is testable
an OAuth token must be generated before authenticated API calls succeed
a service account credential must be provisioned before a cloud SDK authenticates

blocks.condition: The developer must have read and write access to the relevant repository before contributing
examples: push access to the repo must be granted before a PR can be opened
read access must exist before the codebase can be cloned
the developer must be added to the GitHub org before CI secrets are accessible

blocks.condition: The developer must have access to the required environment before they can test, debug, or deploy
examples: SSH access to a staging server must be granted before remote debugging
AWS IAM permissions must be in place before Terraform applies
Kubernetes RBAC must allow the developer to read pod logs before debugging a deployment

blocks.condition: A connection to the required database must be possible before any code that reads or writes data can run
examples: database credentials must be correct before ORM queries succeed
network access to the DB must be open before a connection can be established
the database must be reachable from the test environment before integration tests pass

blocks.condition: Credentials must be stable and not mid-rotation before code that depends on them is run or deployed
examples: a key rotation must be complete before dependent services are restarted
a cert renewal must be done before the old cert expires and services break
API key rotation must be complete and propagated before CI runs that use the new key

CI_CD_STATE.description: What must be true about the CI/CD pipeline before code can be merged or deployed

blocks.condition: All automated checks in the CI pipeline must be green before a PR can be merged
examples: tests must pass before a PR is mergeable
lint and type checks must be clean before merge is allowed
security scan must report no critical issues before CI approves the build

blocks.condition: At least one qualified reviewer must have approved the changes before they are merged
examples: a required reviewer must approve before the merge button is enabled
a security-sensitive change must have a security team approval
an architectural change must be approved by a tech lead

blocks.condition: The environment being deployed to must be in a stable, healthy state before a new release is pushed
examples: staging must not be in a failed state before a new build is deployed to it
the Kubernetes cluster must be healthy before a rollout is triggered
no active incident must be occurring in production before a routine deploy proceeds

blocks.condition: No other deployment must be running before a new one is started, to avoid conflicts
examples: a previous rollout must complete or be rolled back before a new one starts
a database migration must finish before an application deploy begins
a colleague's deployment must succeed or fail before the next deploy proceeds

blocks.condition: The rollback procedure must be verified in a non-production environment before a risky deploy proceeds
examples: a database migration rollback must be tested in staging before applying to prod
the previous Docker tag must be confirmed as runnable before a risky deploy
the feature flag off-switch must be tested before a complex feature is released

ARCHITECTURE_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about the system design before implementation proceeds

blocks.condition: The technical approach must be documented and reviewed before implementation begins
examples: an ADR must be written and approved before a new architectural pattern is adopted
a design doc must be reviewed before a new service is built
an RFC must be accepted before a breaking API change is implemented

blocks.condition: The responsibilities and interfaces of each component must be agreed before individual services are coded
examples: which service owns user data must be decided before the user service is built
the boundary between two services must be agreed before either is implemented
the shared library API must be defined before the library is coded

blocks.condition: Which service or module is the system of record for each piece of data must be agreed before multi-service code is written
examples: the service that owns the user profile must be identified before others read from it
the system of record for an order must be decided before order events are emitted
which database owns a shared entity must be resolved before foreign keys or API calls are coded

blocks.condition: How to avoid breaking existing consumers when changing an interface must be decided before the change is coded
examples: the deprecation strategy for an old field must be agreed before a schema change
the versioning strategy must be chosen before a new API version is built
the migration path for existing data must be planned before a format change is implemented

blocks.condition: Performance, reliability, and scalability targets must be stated before an implementation that must meet them is designed
examples: the target p99 latency must be set before performance-sensitive code is architected
the availability SLA must be agreed before fault tolerance patterns are chosen
the expected peak load must be known before the scaling strategy is designed

SECURITY_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about security posture before code touches sensitive data or systems

blocks.condition: The security risks relevant to the change must be understood before security controls are designed or implemented
examples: the attack surface must be documented before auth code is written
the trust boundaries must be identified before input validation is designed
the sensitive data flows must be mapped before encryption is applied

blocks.condition: How users will authenticate and how permissions will be checked must be agreed before protected endpoints are coded
examples: the token format must be decided before auth middleware is written
the permission model must be agreed before access control checks are implemented
the session management strategy must be chosen before login code is written

blocks.condition: All data fields that require encryption, masking, or special handling must be identified before the data layer is coded
examples: PII fields must be listed before encryption-at-rest is applied
payment card fields must be identified before PCI scope is defined
password fields must be identified before hashing strategy is applied

blocks.condition: A static analysis scan must have been run and its findings understood before new security-sensitive code is added
examples: an initial SAST scan must run before security improvements are measured
the existing findings must be triaged before new ones are introduced
a clean SAST baseline must exist before security gate enforcement begins

blocks.condition: A vulnerability scan of all dependencies must return no critical or high findings before production code is shipped
examples: npm audit must show no critical issues before a release
OWASP dependency check must pass before a Java release
safety check on Python packages must be clean before deployment

OBSERVABILITY_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about instrumentation before a system can be understood in production

blocks.condition: A logging library and output format must be set up before meaningful logs are emitted
examples: a structured logging library must be initialised before log fields are added
log level configuration must be in place before verbosity is controllable
log output must be routed to the expected destination before logs appear in dashboards

blocks.condition: A metrics scrape endpoint or push mechanism must be active before a monitoring system can collect data
examples: a /metrics endpoint must be exposed before Prometheus can scrape
a StatsD emitter must be configured before metrics reach Datadog
OpenTelemetry instrumentation must be initialised before traces are collected

blocks.condition: Alert conditions and notification routes must be configured before an on-call engineer can be paged
examples: a Prometheus alert rule must exist before PagerDuty receives an incident
a threshold must be defined before an anomaly can trigger a notification
an alert routing policy must be set before alerts reach the right team

blocks.condition: Trace IDs must be passed across service boundaries before distributed traces can be correlated
examples: a trace context header must be forwarded before a full request trace is visible
W3C trace context must be injected before Jaeger spans link correctly
correlation IDs must be carried in log fields before cross-service log correlation works

blocks.condition: A service level objective must be documented before compliance with it can be measured or reported
examples: the error rate SLO must be stated before an error budget is calculated
the latency SLO must be published before a burn rate alert is configured
the availability SLO must be agreed before an SLA is signed with a customer

CONCURRENCY_SAFETY.description: What must be true about shared state and synchronisation before concurrent code is safe

blocks.condition: All data that will be accessed by multiple goroutines, threads, or async tasks must be identified before concurrent access patterns are designed
examples: the shared cache must be identified before a mutex is applied
global counters must be listed before atomic operations are introduced
shared request context must be identified before it is made thread-safe

blocks.condition: The approach to protecting shared state must be decided before concurrent code is written
examples: whether to use a mutex or a channel must be decided before goroutine logic is coded
optimistic vs. pessimistic locking must be chosen before the database transaction is written
actor vs. shared memory must be decided before a concurrent data structure is implemented

blocks.condition: Tests that expose unsafe concurrent access must exist before concurrent code is considered correct
examples: a test with -race flag enabled must exist before a concurrent Go function is shipped
a stress test with many parallel requests must run before a concurrent cache is trusted
a concurrent write test must pass before a shared data structure is used in production

blocks.condition: The lock acquisition order must be reviewed for potential deadlocks before multi-lock code is written
examples: the lock order must be documented before two mutexes are acquired together
the cycle analysis must show no circular waits before a lock hierarchy is deployed
a deadlock detector must have run without findings before multi-threaded code ships

blocks.condition: Operations that may be retried must be verified as safe to run multiple times before they are used in retry-capable systems
examples: a payment handler must be idempotent before it is used with a retry queue
a database write must be idempotent before at-least-once message delivery is used
an event handler must be idempotent before it processes from a replayable log

REFACTORING_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before code can be safely restructured

blocks.condition: The code to be refactored must have enough tests to verify that behavior is preserved throughout the change
examples: unit test coverage must be adequate before a function is extracted
integration tests must cover the module before it is restructured
the critical path must be covered by tests before a refactor begins

blocks.condition: What the code is supposed to do must be captured before it is restructured, so correctness can be verified after
examples: a docstring or comment describing the intended behavior must exist before refactoring
acceptance tests that define the expected outputs must pass before the refactor begins
the existing behavior must be characterised with golden tests before it is changed

blocks.condition: Every place that calls the function or module being changed must be located before the refactor begins
examples: all usages of a function must be found before its signature is changed
all importers of a module must be identified before it is renamed or moved
all callers of an API must be mapped before a breaking change is introduced

blocks.condition: The boundaries of the change must be defined so the refactor does not grow uncontrollably
examples: the set of files to be changed must be agreed before work begins
whether to rename across the repo or just within a package must be decided upfront
the depth of restructuring must be bounded before a long refactor session starts

blocks.need: No in-progress work depends on target code
blocks.condition: No other work-in-progress branch should be actively modifying the code being refactored, to avoid expensive merge conflicts
examples: all feature branches touching the target module must be merged or coordinated before the refactor
the team must be aware of the refactor before starting work on related code
no other PR must have an open change to the file being restructured

MIGRATION_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before a migration can be applied safely

blocks.condition: A migration must have run successfully in a non-production environment before it is applied to production
examples: a database migration must succeed in staging before running in prod
an infrastructure change must be validated in a test environment first
a data transformation migration must be verified on a copy of production data

blocks.condition: A script or procedure to reverse the migration must exist and be tested before the forward migration runs
examples: a down migration must exist before the up migration is applied to production
a Terraform destroy plan must be reviewed before apply
a data restoration procedure must be tested before a destructive migration

blocks.condition: A current backup of the data or system being migrated must exist before the migration begins
examples: a database backup must be taken before a schema migration runs
a snapshot must exist before an infrastructure migration
a data export must be saved before a destructive backfill is applied

blocks.condition: The approach for applying the migration without service interruption must be validated before the migration runs on a live system
examples: the online schema change tool must be verified before a large table migration
the expand-contract pattern must be confirmed before a field rename
a canary deploy path must be tested before a breaking API migration

blocks.condition: All downstream consumers of the data or API being migrated must be confirmed as compatible with the new format before migration
examples: all consumers of an event schema must handle the new format before the producer changes
all API clients must support the new response shape before v1 is deprecated
all downstream services must be deployed with compatibility code before a database type change

PERFORMANCE_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true before performance work can be meaningfully attempted

blocks.condition: A specific, measurable goal must exist before optimisation work can be scoped or evaluated
examples: a p99 latency target must be set before profiling begins
a throughput target must be agreed before a concurrency change is designed
a memory budget must be defined before a memory optimisation is measured

blocks.condition: Current performance must be measured under representative conditions before any changes are made
examples: a load test baseline must be captured before optimisation work starts
current query execution times must be recorded before indexes are added
current memory usage under load must be measured before a memory fix is evaluated

blocks.condition: A profile of where time or memory is spent must be collected before the correct optimisation is chosen
examples: a CPU profile must be taken before a hotspot is identified
a heap dump must be captured before a memory leak is traced
a query plan must be examined before an index is added

blocks.condition: Performance testing must use data and load patterns similar to production before results are trusted
examples: a copy of production data must be used before a query optimisation is validated
a representative request distribution must be simulated before a load test is meaningful
production-scale row counts must exist before index selectivity is evaluated

blocks.condition: The system must be stable with no simultaneous changes before performance measurements are attributed to a specific change
examples: no other deploys must be in progress before a before-and-after performance comparison
the environment must be stable before a benchmark is run
no unrelated feature flags must be toggled before a performance regression is confirmed

REVIEW_APPROVAL.description: What must be true before code can be accepted, merged, or released

blocks.condition: The author must have reviewed their own changes for obvious issues before requesting peer review
examples: the developer must have read the diff before opening a PR
the author must have run the tests locally before requesting review
the author must have removed debug output before requesting review

blocks.condition: A clear explanation of what changed and why must be present before reviewers are asked to review
examples: a PR description explaining the approach must exist before review is requested
links to the relevant ticket must be included before review
testing instructions must be written before a reviewer approves the PR

blocks.condition: All blocking review comments must be resolved before a PR is merged
examples: all reviewer comments marked as blocking must be replied to before merge
change requests must be resolved and re-requested for review before merge
unresolved discussions must be explicitly closed or deferred before merge

blocks.condition: All required code owners and stakeholders must have given approval before the merge is permitted
examples: the CODEOWNERS-designated reviewer must approve before merge
security-sensitive files must have a security review approval
the tech lead must approve an architectural change before it merges

blocks.condition: Every automated check registered as required on the branch must be passing before the merge button is enabled
examples: all CI checks must be green before merge is allowed
the required status check must be satisfied before the branch protection allows merge
code coverage threshold must be met before merge is permitted

RELEASE_READINESS.description: What must be true before a version of software is released

blocks.condition: A record of what changed must be written before a release is cut and announced
examples: CHANGELOG.md must be updated before a release tag is created
breaking changes must be documented before a major version is published
a release summary must be written before the GitHub release is created

blocks.condition: The version number must be incremented according to semantic versioning rules before publishing
examples: the patch version must be bumped for a bug fix release
the minor version must be bumped before a backwards-compatible feature release
the major version must be bumped before a breaking change is shipped

blocks.condition: A minimal set of critical-path tests must pass in the release environment before traffic is shifted
examples: a smoke test checking the health endpoint must pass before a prod deploy proceeds
critical user flows must be verified before a canary deploy is widened
a post-deploy check must succeed before old instances are terminated

blocks.condition: The engineer on call must know about a significant release before it goes live so they can monitor and respond
examples: the on-call engineer must be notified before a large deploy
a brief on expected behavioural changes must be shared before a release
runbook updates must be shared before a deploy that changes system behaviour

blocks.condition: Relevant internal and external parties must be informed before a release that affects them
examples: customers must be notified of breaking changes before they are deployed
the support team must be briefed before a UI change goes live
downstream API consumers must be warned before a schema change

UMG_CODING_NEEDS.description: Prerequisites specific to implementing UMG framework constructs in code

blocks.condition: A code representation of the MOLT block structure must exist before a compiler can ingest or process blocks
examples: a Block struct or class with id, type, content, and state fields must exist before ingestion is coded
a MOLT type enum must be defined before type-based authority sorting is implemented
a NeoBlock structure must be defined before the merge stage can produce one

blocks.condition: Code that can read a sleeve definition and expand it into stacks and blocks must exist before the compiler pipeline runs
examples: a JSON sleeve parser must be written before the ingestion stage is tested
module expansion logic must be implemented before the normalization stage runs
sleeve validation must be coded before an invalid sleeve can be rejected with a clear error

blocks.condition: The MOLT type authority hierarchy must be encoded in the compiler before conflict resolution can work correctly
examples: the MOLT type ordering array must be defined before blocks are sorted by authority
the comparison function for MOLT types must be written before the resolution stage
governance block priority must be hardcoded above all other types before the governance pass is trusted

blocks.condition: Code that records decisions to the Decision Trace must exist before the compiler is considered audit-compliant
examples: a TraceEvent struct must be defined before any stage can emit a trace entry
the trace writer must be wired into the governance pass before governance compliance is verified
the full trace must be serialisable before it is passed to an auditor or test

blocks.condition: A test suite that validates compiler behaviour against the UMG canon must exist before a compiler implementation is considered correct
examples: determinism tests must be available before a compiler claims canon compliance
governance supremacy tests must exist before the governance pass is considered correct
trace completeness tests must be defined before a trace implementation is verified as audit-grade

title: need.library.v1.0.0
reason: unsupported role need preserved in legacy only

legacySource.description: Comprehensive library of atomic MOLT Need blocks. Need answers: 'What must be true first?' Each block describes a prerequisite, enabling condition, or required state that must exist before the Aim can be achieved.
legacySource.usage: Select the Need block that best describes what must already be in place for the current Aim to succeed. Need is the enabling condition — if it is absent, the Aim cannot be reached regardless of effort.

DATA_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about data before it can be used

blocks.condition: The required data must exist and be accessible to the process before it can proceed
examples: logs must be present before analysis
user records must exist before profiling
block library must be loaded before compilation

blocks.condition: The data must be correct and substantially free from errors before use
examples: sensor readings must be calibrated
block content must match the authored intent
requirements must reflect what was actually agreed

blocks.condition: The data must be recent enough to reflect current state for the decision at hand
examples: metrics must be from the last 24 hours
block library must be current canon
inventory data must be updated before stock check

blocks.condition: The data must conform to the expected format or structure before it can be processed
examples: JSON must match the expected schema
MOLT block must carry a valid type
CIR must be normalized before resolution

blocks.condition: The process must have explicit permission to read the required data
examples: read role must be granted before query
API key must be scoped to the data endpoint
governance must permit block access before compilation

CODE_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about a codebase or runtime before execution

blocks.condition: A functional execution environment must be present and healthy before code can run
examples: Node.js must be installed and on PATH
Python virtual environment must be active
JVM must be running before app starts

blocks.condition: All required libraries, packages, and modules must be installed and version-compatible
examples: npm install must complete without error
pip packages must be resolved
UMG block references must all exist in the library

blocks.condition: The code to be executed, built, or analyzed must be accessible in the environment
examples: repo must be cloned before build
block definitions must be present before compilation
source files must be readable before lint

blocks.condition: The code must have compiled without errors before it can be tested or deployed
examples: TypeScript must compile clean
CIR must normalize without errors
binary must build before deployment

blocks.condition: A known-good reference state must exist to compare results against
examples: golden output must be stored before regression test
prior compilation trace must exist for comparison
baseline metrics must be recorded before benchmark

SYSTEM_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about infrastructure and environment before a system can operate

blocks.condition: The underlying servers, networks, or cloud resources must be running and reachable
examples: database must be up before app starts
network must be available before API call
cluster must be healthy before deploy

blocks.condition: Network paths between system components must be open, tested, and reachable
examples: VPN must be connected before internal API access
firewall rules must allow traffic
service discovery must resolve before first call

blocks.condition: Authentication tokens, certificates, or keys must be current and correct before access is attempted
examples: OAuth token must not be expired
TLS certificate must be valid
API key must be active and scoped correctly

blocks.condition: Enough compute, storage, memory, or bandwidth must be available before the task is started
examples: disk space must be available before write
memory must be below threshold before heavy job
queue must have capacity before message send

blocks.condition: The system environment must not be in a transitional, degraded, or actively failing state
examples: deployment must not be in progress before traffic shift
database must not be in recovery before query
governance pass must complete before trigger pass starts

CONTENT_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about source material and context before content can be produced or processed

blocks.condition: The content to be processed, summarized, edited, or delivered must exist before work begins
examples: article must be written before it can be summarized
blocks must be authored before sleeve can be compiled
source doc must be uploaded before analysis

blocks.condition: The purpose, audience, and situation for the content must be clear before production begins
examples: user intent must be parsed before response is drafted
MOLT map must be set before output is synthesized
audience level must be known before explanation is written

blocks.condition: The required input or output format must be specified before processing begins
examples: output schema must be defined before rendering
target blueprint must be selected before synthesis
export format must be agreed before report is generated

blocks.condition: Where applicable, required approvals must be in place before content is used or published
examples: governance pass must approve blocks before compilation
legal must sign off before publishing
stakeholder must review before release

blocks.condition: The target language and regional conventions must be defined before content is produced
examples: locale must be set before date formatting
target language must be specified before translation
region must be known before regulatory content is included

PROCESS_PREREQUISITES.description: What must be true about a process or workflow before it can run

blocks.condition: The steps, sequence, and rules of the process must be documented before execution begins
examples: compiler pipeline must be defined before compilation
workflow DAG must be authored before orchestration
deployment runbook must exist before release

blocks.condition: Each step in a process must have a designated owner or executor before the process starts
examples: each governance block must have an author
each stage must have an assigned reviewer
each alert must have a responsible team

blocks.condition: The conditions that start or advance the process must be set and tested before the process runs
examples: CI trigger must be configured before first push
UMG trigger blocks must be authored before evaluation
scheduled job must be registered before first run

blocks.condition: Failure paths and edge cases must have documented responses before the process goes live
examples: error recovery block must be in place before pipeline runs
rollback procedure must exist before deployment
governance fail-fast behavior must be defined before compilation

blocks.condition: The process must have been validated in a safe environment before production use
examples: pipeline must pass integration tests before production deploy
sleeve must be tested before Envoy activation
workflow must run in staging before going live

ACCESS_AUTHORIZATION.description: What must be true about identity and permission before action is permitted

blocks.condition: The user or system must be authenticated before access is granted
examples: login must succeed before dashboard is shown
API client must present valid credentials
governance must confirm caller identity before permitting block access

blocks.condition: The actor must have a defined role that specifies what they are permitted to do
examples: admin role must be assigned before config change
Envoy role must be declared before sleeve activation
reviewer role must be granted before approval action

blocks.condition: Explicit permission for the requested action must exist in the access control system
examples: write permission must exist before file is modified
governance must permit the block before it is compiled
user must have delete right before resource removal

blocks.condition: A valid authenticated session must be in place and unexpired
examples: OAuth session must be valid before protected call
active user session must exist before state mutation
compilation session must be open before CIR is written

blocks.condition: Access must be explicitly limited to the minimum required for the task
examples: API token must be scoped to read-only
governance block must limit tool access before execution
service account must not have broader rights than needed

REQUIREMENT_CLARITY.description: What must be true about requirements and scope before design or build begins

blocks.condition: What must be built or achieved must be written down explicitly before work starts
examples: spec must be written before development begins
MOLT block intent must be stated before authoring
acceptance criteria must exist before test is written

blocks.condition: The conditions for success must be agreed by stakeholders before work begins
examples: UAT criteria must be set before build
MOLT invariants must be defined before compilation
test pass conditions must exist before test suite is run

blocks.condition: The boundaries of what is and is not included must be clear before design begins
examples: in-scope and out-of-scope must be listed before sprint
sleeve domain must be defined before block authoring
patent claim scope must be set before novelty analysis

blocks.condition: All key parties must agree on what is required before design or build proceeds
examples: product and engineering must align on requirements
all Envoy owners must agree on shared canon
governance owner and compiler author must agree on pipeline order

blocks.condition: Unclear or contradictory requirements must be clarified before work proceeds
examples: open ODRs that block design must be resolved first
conflicting block definitions must be reconciled before compilation
ambiguous requirements must be clarified before implementation

PROBLEM_DEFINITION.description: What must be true about a problem before diagnosis or resolution begins

blocks.condition: The issue must be described with enough precision to guide investigation
examples: error message must be available before debugging
governance violation must be named before it can be corrected
user complaint must be understood before a fix is designed

blocks.condition: The boundaries of the problem must be known before diagnosis begins
examples: affected services must be identified before incident response
which blocks are failing must be known before compilation fix
impacted users must be quantified before severity is set

blocks.condition: Observable evidence of the problem must be recorded before root cause analysis begins
examples: error logs must be captured before investigation
trace events must be collected before audit review
user-reported behavior must be noted before reproduction attempt

blocks.condition: A reliable way to trigger the problem must be known before a fix is attempted
examples: steps to reproduce a bug must exist before patch
a sleeve that fails must be available before fix
a governance violation must be reproducible before rule is written

blocks.condition: The severity and scope of the problem must be measurable before priority is set
examples: number of affected users must be known before escalation
data loss extent must be measured before recovery plan
block failure rate must be measured before redesign

TEST_INFRASTRUCTURE.description: What must be true about testing environment and tooling before tests can run

blocks.condition: A dedicated, stable environment for running tests must exist before tests are executed
examples: staging environment must be provisioned
isolated test namespace must be ready
sandbox must be clean before test run

blocks.condition: Representative, compliant input data for tests must be ready before tests run
examples: seed data must be loaded before integration test
mock objects must be configured before unit test
sample sleeves must be available before compiler test

blocks.condition: Specific scenarios to validate must be documented and executable before testing begins
examples: test scripts must be authored before CI run
MOLT invariant checks must be written before audit
acceptance tests must exist before UAT

blocks.condition: A known-good reference state must exist before comparison-based testing begins
examples: golden output must be stored before regression
prior Decision Trace must be saved before re-run
benchmark metrics must be recorded before optimization test

blocks.condition: Required testing frameworks, runners, and libraries must be in place before tests execute
examples: Jest must be installed before unit test run
test harness must be configured before sleeve audit
coverage tool must be present before measurement

CONFIGURATION_STATE.description: What must be true about configuration and settings before a system can operate correctly

blocks.condition: All settings and parameters must be recorded and accessible before operation begins
examples: config file must exist before service start
MOLT block parameters must be declared before compilation
environment manifest must be present before provisioning

blocks.condition: Required runtime variables must be present and correct in the environment before execution
examples: DATABASE_URL must be set before app start
GOVERNANCE_MODE must be declared before compilation
API_KEY must be injected before first call

blocks.condition: Credentials, keys, and tokens must be securely available to the process before it runs
examples: vault secrets must be fetched before service start
TLS keys must be mounted before HTTPS listener
signing key must be available before token issuance

blocks.condition: The correct feature toggles must be in the expected state before the system executes
examples: governance strict mode must be enabled before audit
experimental block must be flagged OFF before production
live compilation must be toggled on before interactive session

blocks.condition: The configuration in use must be traceable to a specific documented version before deployment
examples: config must be committed to source control before deploy
canon version must be confirmed before compilation
infrastructure manifest must be tagged before apply

COMMUNICATION_CHANNEL.description: What must be true about channels and protocols before communication can occur

blocks.condition: The communication pathway must be active and reachable before a message is sent
examples: WebSocket must be connected before event push
SMTP server must be reachable before email send
API endpoint must be listening before request

blocks.condition: Both parties must use the same communication format or standard before exchange begins
examples: REST contract must be agreed before first API call
MOLT schema version must match before block exchange
message format must be aligned before queue publish

blocks.condition: The target of the message must be available to receive before sending
examples: service must be healthy before routing to it
user must be active before push notification
Envoy must be initialized before receiving a MOLT block

blocks.condition: The channel must have capacity for the required message volume before traffic is sent
examples: queue must not be full before new publish
bandwidth must be available before file transfer
governance channel must not be saturated before audit send

blocks.condition: The message must conform to what the recipient expects before delivery
examples: JSON must be well-formed before parse
MOLT block must be correctly typed before ingestion
API body must match OpenAPI spec before acceptance

STRUCTURAL_FOUNDATION.description: What must be true about models, schemas, and structures before reasoning can proceed

blocks.condition: A formal structure for data or blocks must be established before authoring or processing begins
examples: MOLT block schema must be defined before authoring library
database schema must exist before migration
API response schema must be agreed before client is built

blocks.condition: The structural model must be internally consistent and correct before it is relied upon
examples: domain model must pass consistency checks before build
sleeve must be validated before Envoy activation
data model must be reviewed before production use

blocks.condition: Categories and classifications must be agreed and documented before things are organized into them
examples: MOLT type hierarchy must be defined before blocks are typed
permission levels must be named before roles are assigned
product categories must exist before items are cataloged

blocks.condition: Connections between entities must be defined and documented before reasoning about them proceeds
examples: block dependencies must be mapped before compilation
service dependencies must be known before load test
team dependencies must be understood before scheduling

blocks.condition: A single authoritative version of the structure must be designated before any work uses it
examples: canon document must be the source of truth before implementation
master branch must be designated before merge
primary block definition must be identified before synthesis

RESOURCE_AVAILABILITY.description: What must be true about resources before work can proceed

blocks.condition: Sufficient processing capacity must be allocated for the task before it starts
examples: CPU quota must not be exhausted before job starts
inference budget must be available before compilation
cluster capacity must exist before deploy

blocks.condition: Enough storage for inputs, outputs, and state must be reserved before the process runs
examples: disk space must be confirmed before log write
S3 bucket must exist before artifact upload
CIR storage must be available before compilation

blocks.condition: Financial resources must be authorized before spending is committed
examples: project budget must be approved before tooling purchase
API cost must be estimated before high-volume call
infrastructure cost must be cleared before provisioning

blocks.condition: A sufficient window must exist to complete the task before the deadline
examples: deployment window must be open before release
review cycle must fit within sprint before ship
compilation must complete within session timeout

blocks.condition: Required people must be available and committed before work is scheduled to begin
examples: reviewer must be assigned before PR is submitted
governance owner must be named before policy is enacted
Envoy operator must be available before sleeve activation

INTERFACE_CONTRACT.description: What must be true about interfaces and contracts before systems can connect

blocks.condition: The interface specification must be agreed and published before any client or server implements it
examples: OpenAPI spec must be finalized before SDK is built
RuntimeSpec schema must be stable before executor is coded
MOLT block interface must be defined before first authoring tool

blocks.condition: What the system expects to receive must be precisely defined before data is sent
examples: request body schema must be published before client sends
sleeve format must be specified before compiler reads it
trigger activation pattern must be defined before input is mapped

blocks.condition: What the system will produce must be precisely defined before consumers depend on it
examples: RuntimeSpec format must be stable before executor relies on it
report structure must be agreed before report is built
Decision Trace schema must be published before auditor reads it

blocks.condition: How failures will be signaled and handled must be documented before the interface is used
examples: error codes must be defined before client handles them
governance fail-fast behavior must be specified before compilation
retry semantics must be agreed before queue consumer is built

blocks.condition: The specific interface version in use must be locked before work that depends on it begins
examples: API version must be pinned in client config
canon version must be confirmed before compilation
library version must be locked in package manifest

OBSERVABILITY_BASELINE.description: What must be true about logging, metrics, and monitoring before a system goes live

blocks.condition: The system must be configured to record its actions and state changes before it handles real traffic
examples: application logging must be on before production deploy
Decision Trace logging must be active before compilation
audit log must be enabled before sensitive actions are taken

blocks.condition: Quantitative measures of system behavior must be captured before performance can be assessed
examples: response time instrumentation must be in place before load test
block compilation counts must be emitted before analysis
error rates must be tracked before SLA is evaluated

blocks.condition: Requests must be traceable from entry to exit across all components before debugging is possible
examples: distributed trace IDs must be propagated before correlation
MOLT compilation trace must be written before audit
request span must be started before downstream calls

blocks.condition: Thresholds and notification rules must be set before a system is relied upon in production
examples: error rate alert must be configured before release
governance violation alert must be active before deployment
capacity warning must be set before high-load event

blocks.condition: Normal system behavior must be characterized before deviations can be detected as anomalies
examples: baseline metrics must be recorded before anomaly detection is trusted
expected compilation time must be profiled before regression alert
normal error rate must be known before alerting threshold is set

POLICY_DEFINITION.description: What must be true about policies and rules before they can be enforced

blocks.condition: The governing rules must be written in explicit, inspectable form before enforcement begins
examples: governance block must be authored before governance pass runs
data retention policy must be written before it is enforced
access control rules must be declared before permission checks

blocks.condition: The rules must be made available to all parties subject to them before enforcement
examples: governance stack must be visible to all Envoys before compilation
data policy must be published before it is binding
rate limit rules must be documented before API is used

blocks.condition: Those governed by the policy must know what it requires of them before they can comply
examples: Envoy must understand which blocks are OFF before composing a sleeve
team must understand GDPR obligations before handling data
user must know rate limits before building an integration

blocks.condition: A mechanism that applies the policy and detects violations must be operating before the system handles real inputs
examples: governance pass must be running before compilation
rate limiter must be active before API is public
access control layer must be deployed before traffic is routed

blocks.condition: Changes to policy must be tracked with clear versioning before multiple versions can coexist or evolve
examples: governance stack must be tagged before canon update
policy document must be committed to source control
MOLT invariants must be versioned before v1 is declared

DATA_QUALITY_BASELINE.description: What must be true about data quality before analysis or decisions can rely on it

blocks.condition: All required fields, records, and elements must be present before the data is used
examples: all MOLT types must be populated before sleeve is compiled
all required form fields must be filled before submission
all test cases must be present before coverage is measured

blocks.condition: Data must not contain internal contradictions within or across sources before it is trusted
examples: block definitions must not conflict before library is used
config values must not contradict each other before deploy
governance rules must not conflict before enforcement

blocks.condition: Redundant records must be identified and eliminated before analysis produces reliable results
examples: duplicate blocks must be resolved before library is published
duplicate events must be deduplicated before metrics are calculated
duplicate requirements must be merged before spec is finalized

blocks.condition: The origin and chain of custody of data must be known before it is relied upon for decisions
examples: block source must be traceable before it is used in governance
data lineage must be documented before regulatory report
Decision Trace must record block origin before audit

blocks.condition: Data must fall within the acceptable age window for the use case before it is used
examples: block library must be current canon version before compilation
market data must be from today before trading decision
security scan results must be recent before deployment approval

DELIVERY_READINESS.description: What must be true about delivery pipelines and processes before releasing

blocks.condition: The deployment or delivery mechanism must be running and tested before it carries production traffic
examples: CI/CD pipeline must be passing before merge to main
compilation pipeline must complete without error before RuntimeSpec is trusted
delivery channel must be tested before release

blocks.condition: A way to undo the delivery must exist and be tested before proceeding
examples: previous version must be tagged before new deploy
governance rollback procedure must be documented before rule change
database migration must be reversible before apply

blocks.condition: Those affected by the delivery must be informed in advance before it proceeds
examples: users must be notified of maintenance window before deploy
downstream Envoys must be informed of canon change before it is released
affected teams must be briefed before a breaking API change

blocks.condition: All required sign-offs must be in place before the release proceeds
examples: security review must be signed off before production deploy
governance owner must approve rule change before enforcement
product must sign off on feature before ship

blocks.condition: Pre-delivery validation checks must all succeed before the release proceeds
examples: all tests must pass before merge
all MOLT invariants must be satisfied before RuntimeSpec is emitted
all canary checks must pass before full rollout

DEPENDENCY_RESOLUTION.description: What must be true about dependencies before a component can function

blocks.condition: Every component, service, library, or block that is required must be listed before work begins
examples: all block references in a sleeve must be listed before compilation
all service dependencies must be known before architecture review
all package requirements must be declared in manifest before install

blocks.condition: All dependency versions must be mutually compatible before they are used together
examples: block library version must be compatible with compiler version
package versions must not have peer conflicts
canon version must be agreed across all Envoys before mesh sync

blocks.condition: No dependency must ultimately require itself to function before the system is assembled
examples: block must not reference itself as a dependency
service must not depend on itself through a chain
MOLT type must not require itself in its resolution

blocks.condition: Third-party services, APIs, or libraries must be available before the system starts
examples: npm registry must be reachable before install
external API must be up before integration test
external block library must be accessible before compilation

blocks.condition: Legal obligations of all dependencies must be understood before they are included in a product
examples: open source licenses must be checked before distribution
third-party block licenses must be reviewed before ClawHub publication
SDK terms must be accepted before commercial use

UMG_FRAMEWORK_NEEDS.description: Prerequisites specific to UMG compilation, governance, and Envoy operation

blocks.condition: The governance stack must be written and canon-valid before any compilation pass begins
examples: governance NeoStack must be defined before sleeve is compiled
OFF states must be declared before governance pass runs
governance rules must be authored before Envoy activation

blocks.condition: The target sleeve must be expanded and normalized into CIR before resolution begins
examples: sleeve must be ingested and modules expanded before normalization
CIR must be produced before governance pass runs
sleeve must be valid before trigger evaluation begins

blocks.condition: The blocks referenced in the sleeve must exist and be accessible before compilation proceeds
examples: all MOLT blocks must be in the library before sleeve is compiled
NeoBlock sources must be resolvable before merge
governance block definitions must be accessible before governance pass

blocks.condition: Every block must carry a valid MOLT type before authority resolution can proceed
examples: all blocks must be typed before compiler sorts by authority
untyped blocks must be rejected before CIR is produced
MOLT type must be declared before conflict site is evaluated

blocks.condition: The UMG compiler must be ready, with canon version confirmed, before a compilation pass is started
examples: compiler canon version must match sleeve spec version before compile
compiler must be initialized before sleeve ingestion
compiler state must be clean before each compilation session

block.label: Subordinate To UMG Governance
block.text: LangChain is an adapter engine and execution substrate; UMG remains the operating grammar, governance layer, provenance layer, and sleeve composition authority.

library.name: MOLT PHILOSOPHY Library
library.description: Normalized philosophy catalog produced during Stage 4

entries.name: Platonism
entries.core_principles: Reality of eternal Forms/Ideas beyond physical world; knowledge through reason and dialectic; material world as imperfect reflection of ideal Forms
entries.application: Seek underlying ideal principles beneath surface appearances; prioritize abstract reasoning over empirical observation; look for perfect forms that material instances approximate

entries.name: Aristotelianism
entries.core_principles: Knowledge through observation and logic; virtue as mean between extremes; teleology (purpose-driven nature); empirical investigation
entries.application: Ground reasoning in observation and evidence; seek purpose and function; find balanced middle path; use logical categorization and analysis

entries.name: Stoicism
entries.core_principles: Virtue as sole good; acceptance of what cannot be controlled; reason as guide; living according to nature; equanimity in face of fortune
entries.application: Focus on what is within control; accept external circumstances calmly; prioritize virtue over outcomes; maintain rational perspective on emotions

entries.name: Epicureanism
entries.core_principles: Pleasure (especially absence of pain) as highest good; atomistic materialism; moderate enjoyment; friendship and simple living
entries.application: Seek sustainable pleasure and pain avoidance; value tranquility over intensity; reduce unnecessary desires; prioritize lasting contentment

entries.name: Cynicism
entries.core_principles: Virtue through ascetic living; rejection of social conventions; self-sufficiency; living according to nature; shameless honesty
entries.application: Question social norms and conventions; prioritize authenticity over social acceptance; embrace simplicity; speak truth regardless of comfort

entries.name: Skepticism
entries.core_principles: Suspension of judgment on non-evident matters; questioning dogmatic claims; recognition of limits of knowledge; epoché (withholding assent)
entries.application: Question assumptions; acknowledge uncertainty; avoid dogmatic claims; suspend judgment when evidence insufficient; maintain intellectual humility

entries.name: Pre-Socratic Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Naturalistic explanations; search for fundamental substance (arche); rational inquiry into nature; cosmological speculation
entries.application: Seek natural rather than supernatural explanations; look for fundamental principles underlying phenomena; use reason to understand cosmos

entries.name: Sophistry
entries.core_principles: Rhetoric and persuasion; relativism of truth; practical skill in argument; teaching as profession; contextual truth
entries.application: Adapt arguments to audience; recognize perspective-dependent truth; prioritize persuasive effectiveness; value rhetorical skill

entries.name: Neoplatonism
