entries.core_principles: The One as ultimate reality; emanation of being; mystical union; hierarchical reality from spiritual to material; contemplation as path to divine
entries.application: Seek unity underlying multiplicity; move from material to spiritual understanding; value contemplation; recognize hierarchical ordering of reality

entries.name: Pyrrhonism
entries.core_principles: Radical doubt; opposing arguments of equal strength; suspension of all beliefs; ataraxia through skepticism; appearance vs reality distinction
entries.application: For every claim find equally strong counter-claim; suspend belief on disputed matters; seek tranquility through non-commitment; distinguish appearance from claimed reality

entries.name: Atomism
entries.core_principles: Reality composed of indivisible atoms and void; mechanical causation; no teleology; materialist ontology; emergent properties
entries.application: Explain phenomena through material composition and motion; avoid purpose-based explanations; reduce complex to simple elements; mechanical interactions

entries.name: Hedonism
entries.core_principles: Pleasure as intrinsic good; pain as intrinsic evil; maximization of pleasure; value in experience itself
entries.application: Evaluate actions by pleasure produced; prioritize experiential quality; seek positive states; minimize suffering

entries.name: Asceticism
entries.core_principles: Self-discipline and abstinence; spiritual purification through material denial; mastery over bodily desires; simple living
entries.application: Resist immediate gratification; practice self-denial; purify through discipline; minimize material attachments; strengthen will

entries.name: Eudaimonism
entries.core_principles: Human flourishing as highest good; virtue and reason as path to happiness; excellent functioning; living well
entries.application: Aim for holistic flourishing not just pleasure; develop virtues; exercise reason excellently; achieve full human potential

entries.name: Peripatetic Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Aristotle's comprehensive system; walking while teaching; empirical observation; logical analysis; Golden Mean; four causes
entries.application: Combine observation with logical analysis; seek moderation; identify material, formal, efficient, and final causes; systematic categorization

entries.name: Megaric School
entries.core_principles: Focus on logic and dialectic; paradoxes and puzzles; denial of potentiality; only actuality exists; logical rigor
entries.application: Use logical analysis rigorously; explore paradoxes; focus on what is actual rather than potential; precise definitions and distinctions

entries.name: Eleatic School
entries.core_principles: Being is one, unchanging, eternal; change and multiplicity are illusion; logical argument over sensory evidence; Parmenides' way of truth
entries.application: Trust reason over senses; see beneath apparent change to underlying permanence; logical consistency paramount; unity over multiplicity

entries.name: Pythagorean Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Number as essence of reality; mathematical harmony; transmigration of souls; purification through knowledge; mystical mathematics
entries.application: Seek mathematical patterns in phenomena; value numerical relationships; see harmony and proportion; combine mysticism with mathematics

entries.name: Academic Skepticism
entries.core_principles: Plato's Academy skeptical period; probability over certainty; practical criterion without absolute knowledge; against Stoic certainty claims
entries.application: Seek probable rather than certain knowledge; act on reasonable beliefs without claiming certainty; critique dogmatic assertions

entries.name: Sophism
entries.core_principles: Teaching rhetoric and virtue for fee; relativism about truth and morality; human measure of all things; persuasive technique
entries.application: Recognize contextual nature of claims; adapt to audience and situation; value persuasive skill; pragmatic approach to truth

entries.name: Buddhism
entries.core_principles: Four Noble Truths (suffering exists, has cause, can cease, path to cessation); impermanence; no-self; Middle Way; compassion
entries.application: Recognize impermanence of all things; see suffering arising from attachment; seek cessation through right practice; cultivate compassion

entries.name: Taoism
entries.core_principles: Wu wei (effortless action); harmony with Dao; naturalness over convention; yin-yang complementarity; simplicity
entries.application: Act without forcing; flow with natural processes; embrace simplicity; balance opposites; minimal intervention yields best results

entries.name: Confucianism
entries.core_principles: Ritual propriety (li); filial piety; social harmony; virtue cultivation; education and self-improvement; reciprocity
entries.application: Maintain proper relationships; cultivate virtue through practice; value education and tradition; seek social harmony; reciprocal treatment

entries.name: Hinduism (Vedanta)
entries.core_principles: Brahman as ultimate reality; atman (self) identical with Brahman; moksha (liberation); karma and dharma; maya (illusion)
entries.application: Seek union with ultimate reality; see beyond apparent multiplicity to underlying unity; fulfill dharma; transcend illusion

entries.name: Zen Buddhism
entries.core_principles: Direct insight beyond words; meditation (zazen); koans; sudden enlightenment; present moment awareness; non-dualism
entries.application: Look beyond conceptual thinking; value direct experience; be fully present; see paradoxes as pointing beyond logic; practice mindfulness

entries.name: Advaita Vedanta
entries.core_principles: Non-dualism; Brahman alone is real; world appearance not ultimate reality; self-inquiry; knowledge destroys ignorance
entries.application: See unity underlying apparent diversity; question subject-object duality; recognize consciousness as fundamental; use inquiry to dispel illusion

entries.name: Jainism
entries.core_principles: Non-violence (ahimsa); many-sided truth (anekantavada); non-absolutism; karma as material; soul purification; ascetic path
entries.application: Practice strict non-violence; recognize multiple valid perspectives; avoid absolute claims; purify through discipline; respect all life

entries.name: Yoga Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Eight-fold path; union of individual with divine; control of mind modifications; practice and detachment; concentration and meditation
entries.application: Cultivate mental discipline; practice systematically; combine effort with detachment; progress through stages; aim for union/integration

entries.name: Legalism (Chinese)
entries.core_principles: Rule by law not virtue; rewards and punishments; realism about human nature; centralized authority; practical statecraft
entries.application: Establish clear rules and consequences; assume self-interest not virtue; centralize control; prioritize order and stability; pragmatic governance

entries.name: Mohism
entries.core_principles: Universal love (impartial caring); utilitarian ethics; meritocracy; anti-fatalism; pragmatic logic; frugality
entries.application: Care equally for all; evaluate by consequences; promote capability over birth; reject fatalism; use resources efficiently; logical clarity

entries.name: School of Names
entries.core_principles: Logic and language analysis; relationship between names and reality; paradoxes; distinction and argumentation; semantic precision
entries.application: Analyze language carefully; distinguish naming from reality; explore logical relationships; precise definitions; examine paradoxes

entries.name: Neo-Confucianism
entries.core_principles: Li (principle) and qi (vital energy); investigation of things; moral cultivation; synthesis of Confucianism with Buddhist/Taoist elements
entries.application: Investigate underlying principles; cultivate moral nature; integrate contemplation with action; seek harmony of principle and energy

entries.name: Samkhya
entries.core_principles: Dualism of consciousness (purusha) and matter (prakriti); evolution of world from prakriti; liberation through discrimination; enumeration of categories
entries.application: Distinguish consciousness from material processes; recognize evolutionary unfolding; achieve liberation through discrimination; systematic categorization

entries.name: Nyaya
entries.core_principles: Logic and epistemology; valid means of knowledge; logical inference; debate methodology; liberation through right knowledge
entries.application: Use rigorous logical inference; identify valid knowledge sources; engage in structured debate; systematic argumentation; epistemological clarity

entries.name: Vaisheshika
entries.core_principles: Atomism; categories of reality (substance, quality, action, etc.); particularist ontology; natural causation
entries.application: Analyze reality through categorical distinctions; recognize atomic composition; natural causal explanations; systematic classification

entries.name: Mimamsa
entries.core_principles: Vedic ritual authority; dharma through proper action; hermeneutical analysis; eternal Vedas; ritual efficacy
entries.application: Ground ethics in established texts; careful textual interpretation; ritual precision; action-based dharma; systematic exegesis

entries.name: Chan Buddhism
entries.core_principles: Sudden enlightenment; Buddha-nature in all; meditation; direct transmission; non-reliance on scriptures; everyday mind
entries.application: Recognize inherent Buddha-nature; value direct experience; see sacred in ordinary; sudden insight over gradual; meditation in action

entries.name: Pure Land Buddhism
entries.core_principles: Faith in Amitabha Buddha; rebirth in Pure Land; other-power over self-power; accessible path; devotional practice
entries.application: Rely on grace and faith; value accessibility; devotional approach; trust in other-power; compassionate assistance

entries.name: Madhyamaka
entries.core_principles: Middle way between extremes; emptiness (śūnyatā) of inherent existence; dependent origination; two truths (conventional and ultimate)
entries.application: Avoid extreme positions; recognize emptiness of inherent existence; see dependent arising; distinguish conventional from ultimate truth

entries.name: Yogacara
entries.core_principles: Consciousness-only; mind as foundation; transformation of consciousness; storehouse consciousness; phenomenology of experience
entries.application: Recognize mind as basis of experience; transform consciousness; examine mental processes; phenomenological analysis; idealist ontology

entries.name: Scholasticism
entries.core_principles: Faith and reason synthesis; Aristotelian logic applied to theology; systematic argumentation; university method; dialectical reasoning
entries.application: Integrate faith and reason; use logical argumentation; systematic theology; resolve apparent contradictions; dialectical method

entries.name: Augustinianism
entries.core_principles: Divine illumination for knowledge; original sin; grace over works; introspection; faith seeking understanding; two cities
entries.application: Seek divine illumination; recognize human limitation; value grace; introspective examination; faith as prerequisite for understanding

entries.name: Thomism
entries.core_principles: Aristotelian Christianity; analogy of being; five ways to prove God; natural law; act and potency; essence and existence distinction
entries.application: Use Aristotelian categories theologically; natural theology; analogical reasoning; natural law ethics; systematic metaphysics

entries.name: Islamic Philosophy (Falsafa)
entries.core_principles: Reason and revelation compatibility; Greek philosophy integrated with Islam; emanation; active intellect; metaphysical speculation
entries.application: Harmonize philosophy with religious truth; rational theology; metaphysical analysis; emanationist cosmology; philosophical inquiry

entries.name: Sufism
entries.core_principles: Islamic mysticism; direct experience of divine; stages of spiritual path; annihilation in God; esoteric knowledge; love of divine
entries.application: Seek direct divine experience; follow spiritual stages; transcend ego; esoteric interpretation; devotional practice; mystical insight

entries.name: Jewish Philosophy (Maimonides)
entries.core_principles: Rationalism in Judaism; reconciling Torah with Aristotle; negative theology; guide for perplexed; philosophical interpretation
entries.application: Rational examination of faith; allegorical interpretation; negative description of God; philosophical coherence; integration of reason

entries.name: Christian Mysticism
entries.core_principles: Direct union with God; contemplative prayer; purgative-illuminative-unitive stages; dark night; ineffable experience
entries.application: Seek divine union through contemplation; progress through spiritual stages; value ineffable experience; apophatic approach

entries.name: Nominalism
entries.core_principles: Universals are names only; only particulars exist; Ockham's razor; radical divine freedom; empirical emphasis
entries.application: Focus on particulars over universals; simplify explanations; recognize conventional nature of categories; divine voluntarism

entries.name: Realism (Medieval)
entries.core_principles: Universals exist independently; Platonic forms in divine mind; intelligible essences; ante rem (before things) reality
entries.application: Recognize reality of universals; intelligible essences; forms in divine intellect; realist ontology; essential natures

entries.name: Voluntarism
entries.core_principles: Will over intellect; Duns Scotus; divine will primary; individual essence (haecceity); contingency emphasis
entries.application: Prioritize will over intellect; recognize radical contingency; individual uniqueness; voluntarist metaphysics; freedom emphasis

entries.name: Rationalism
entries.core_principles: Knowledge through reason alone; innate ideas; deductive method; clear and distinct ideas; mathematical model
entries.application: Trust reason over senses; deduce from first principles; seek certainty through clear ideas; mathematical reasoning; systematic doubt

entries.name: Empiricism
entries.core_principles: Knowledge through experience; tabula rasa; sensory evidence; inductive method; skepticism of innate ideas
entries.application: Ground claims in observation; inductive reasoning; sensory evidence paramount; reject unobservable speculation; experimental verification

entries.name: Idealism
entries.core_principles: Mind/ideas as fundamental reality; esse est percipi (to be is to be perceived); rejection of material substance; phenomenalism
entries.application: Recognize mind as primary; ideas as fundamental; reject mind-independent matter; phenomenological focus; consciousness-centered

entries.name: Materialism
entries.core_principles: Matter as fundamental; physical processes generate mental; reductionism; mechanistic causation; naturalism
entries.application: Explain phenomena through material processes; reduce mental to physical; mechanistic explanations; naturalistic worldview

entries.name: Dualism
entries.core_principles: Mind and body as distinct substances; interaction problem; res cogitans and res extensa; irreducible mental properties
entries.application: Recognize mind-body distinction; mental causation; non-reducible consciousness; substance dualism or property dualism

entries.name: Kantian Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Transcendental idealism; synthetic a priori knowledge; categorical imperative; phenomena vs noumena; autonomy as freedom
entries.application: Recognize mind's structuring of experience; moral duty over consequences; respect persons as ends; critical examination of reason's limits

entries.name: Hegelianism
entries.core_principles: Dialectical process (thesis-antithesis-synthesis); absolute idealism; Spirit's self-realization; historical development; unity of opposites
entries.application: See reality as dialectical process; recognize contradictions as productive; historical development toward absolute; systematic totality

entries.name: Utilitarianism
entries.core_principles: Greatest happiness for greatest number; consequentialism; pleasure and pain as metrics; impartial benevolence; hedonic calculus
entries.application: Evaluate by consequences; maximize overall happiness; impartial consideration; quantify pleasure/pain where possible; aggregate welfare

entries.name: Pragmatism
entries.core_principles: Truth as what works; meaning in practical consequences; anti-foundationalism; experimental attitude; pluralism
entries.application: Test ideas by practical results; focus on consequences not origins; experimental approach; multiple valid perspectives; actionable truth

entries.name: Phenomenology
entries.core_principles: Study of consciousness and experience; bracketing (epoché); intentionality; lived experience; essences through variation
entries.application: Describe experience directly; suspend theoretical assumptions; recognize intentional structure; focus on phenomena as given; essential structures

entries.name: Existentialism
entries.core_principles: Existence precedes essence; radical freedom; authenticity; anxiety and absurdity; individual responsibility; subjective truth
entries.application: Recognize radical freedom; create own essence through choice; authentic existence; face anxiety; personal responsibility; subjective meaning

entries.name: Marxism
entries.core_principles: Historical materialism; class struggle; alienation under capitalism; dialectical materialism; revolutionary praxis
entries.application: Analyze through class relations; recognize material conditions shaping consciousness; critique ideology; revolutionary change; praxis over theory alone

entries.name: Positivism
entries.core_principles: Only scientific knowledge valid; rejection of metaphysics; observable facts; law-like generalizations; unity of science
entries.application: Limit claims to observable; scientific method paramount; reject metaphysical speculation; empirical verification; law discovery

entries.name: Logical Positivism
entries.core_principles: Verification principle; analytic-synthetic distinction; protocol sentences; logical analysis of language; unified science
entries.application: Verify claims empirically or analytically; logical analysis of statements; reject unverifiable metaphysics; formal precision; scientific language

entries.name: Naturalism
entries.core_principles: Nature as all that exists; scientific method as sole reliable path; rejection of supernatural; continuity with natural sciences
entries.application: Naturalistic explanations only; scientific methodology; reject supernatural causes; continuous with empirical sciences; physicalist ontology

entries.name: Social Contract Theory
entries.core_principles: Political legitimacy from agreement; state of nature hypothetical; consent as foundation; individual rights; limited government
entries.application: Ground political authority in consent; hypothetical social contract; protect individual rights; government serves people; constitutional limits

entries.name: Liberalism
entries.core_principles: Individual liberty and rights; limited government; rule of law; free markets; tolerance; equality of opportunity
entries.application: Protect individual freedom; constrain state power; legal equality; market mechanisms; toleration of diversity; rights protection

entries.name: Conservatism
entries.core_principles: Tradition and custom; gradual change; skepticism of radical reform; organic society; prudence; established institutions
entries.application: Value tradition and precedent; incremental change; skeptical of utopian schemes; preserve functioning institutions; practical wisdom

entries.name: Romanticism
entries.core_principles: Emotion and intuition; nature as spiritual; individual genius; imagination over reason; authentic expression; sublime experience
entries.application: Value emotional and imaginative insight; spiritual connection to nature; individual creativity; authentic expression; sublime and beautiful

entries.name: German Idealism
entries.core_principles: Post-Kantian idealism; self-positing subject; freedom and autonomy; systematic philosophy; dialectical development
entries.application: Self-conscious subject as fundamental; freedom as essence; systematic philosophical construction; dialectical method; idealist metaphysics

entries.name: Analytic Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Logical analysis; conceptual clarity; argument precision; language analysis; scientific philosophy
entries.application: Analyze concepts clearly; rigorous argumentation; logical precision; language focus; scientific approach to philosophy

entries.name: Continental Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Phenomenology, existentialism, post-structuralism; hermeneutics; historical consciousness; human condition; interpretive methods
entries.application: Interpret texts and experience; historical situatedness; existential concerns; hermeneutical approach; human meaning

entries.name: Structuralism
entries.core_principles: Deep structures underlying phenomena; linguistic model; binary oppositions; synchronic analysis; systems of relations
entries.application: Identify underlying structures; relational analysis; binary oppositions; systematic relationships; structural explanations

entries.name: Post-Structuralism
entries.core_principles: Deconstruction; différance; critique of presence; multiplicity of meaning; textual play; anti-foundationalism
entries.application: Deconstruct binary oppositions; recognize multiple meanings; critique foundations; textual analysis; defer final meaning

entries.name: Postmodernism
entries.core_principles: Skepticism of grand narratives; perspectivism; fragmentation; irony; anti-essentialism; local knowledge
entries.application: Question meta-narratives; multiple perspectives; local and contextual knowledge; ironic stance; anti-essentialist approach

entries.name: Feminism
entries.core_principles: Gender analysis; critique of patriarchy; situated knowledge; intersectionality; care ethics; equality and justice
entries.application: Analyze gender dynamics; critique male-centered assumptions; recognize situated perspectives; intersectional analysis; ethics of care

entries.name: Critical Theory
entries.core_principles: Frankfurt School; ideology critique; emancipatory knowledge; reason and domination; dialectical social theory
entries.application: Critique ideology and power structures; emancipatory aims; dialectical analysis; reason's instrumental turn; social transformation

entries.name: Hermeneutics
entries.core_principles: Interpretation theory; hermeneutic circle; understanding and application; fusion of horizons; textual meaning
entries.application: Interpret through circular process; understand in context; apply understanding; fuse historical horizons; meaningful interpretation

entries.name: Ordinary Language Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Everyday language use; dissolve philosophical problems through linguistic analysis; language games; family resemblances
entries.application: Analyze ordinary usage; dissolve pseudo-problems; recognize language games; avoid abstract theorizing; practical linguistic philosophy

entries.name: Philosophy of Mind
entries.core_principles: Consciousness studies; mental causation; mind-body problem; qualia; intentionality; cognitive science integration
entries.application: Analyze consciousness and mental states; address mind-body relation; study intentionality; integrate cognitive science; mental causation

entries.name: Personalism
entries.core_principles: Irreducible value of persons; dignity; relation and community; against depersonalization; person as fundamental category
entries.application: Recognize inherent personal dignity; relational ontology; resist reduction of persons; community and relation; personal freedom

entries.name: Communitarianism
entries.core_principles: Community over individual; situated self; common good; tradition and practice; critique of abstract individualism
entries.application: Prioritize communal good; recognize social embeddedness; value tradition; common goods; situated moral reasoning

entries.name: Virtue Ethics
entries.core_principles: Character over rules; Aristotelian revival; virtues and vices; eudaimonia; practical wisdom (phronesis); role models
entries.application: Cultivate virtues; practical wisdom; character development; emulate exemplars; holistic moral formation; flourishing

entries.name: Consequentialism
entries.core_principles: Moral value in outcomes; maximize good consequences; impartial consideration; aggregative ethics; forward-looking
entries.application: Evaluate by results; maximize overall good; impartial perspective; consequential thinking; aggregate welfare

entries.name: Deontology
entries.core_principles: Duty-based ethics; categorical imperative; respect for persons; rule-following; moral law; backward-looking justification
entries.application: Follow moral duties; treat persons as ends; universal rules; moral law; respect autonomy; duty over consequences

entries.name: Libertarianism
entries.core_principles: Maximum individual liberty; minimal state; property rights; self-ownership; free markets; non-aggression principle
entries.application: Maximize freedom; minimal government intervention; protect property rights; voluntary exchange; non-coercion

entries.name: Anarchism
entries.core_principles: Rejection of hierarchical authority; voluntary association; mutual aid; direct action; anti-statism; horizontal organization
entries.application: Reject coercive hierarchies; voluntary cooperation; mutual aid; direct action; decentralized organization; anti-authoritarianism

entries.name: Nihilism
entries.core_principles: Rejection of objective meaning and values; existential vacuum; death of God; radical skepticism; meaninglessness
entries.application: Recognize absence of inherent meaning; reject objective values; existential honesty; confronting meaninglessness; radical questioning

entries.name: Absurdism
entries.core_principles: Life's inherent meaninglessness; revolt against absurdity; embrace the absurd; Sisyphus as model; living without appeal
entries.application: Acknowledge absurdity; revolt through passionate living; no false hope; embrace contradiction; create personal meaning despite meaninglessness

entries.name: Process Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Reality as process not substance; becoming over being; Whitehead's actual occasions; creativity; relational ontology
entries.application: See reality as dynamic process; becoming fundamental; creative advance; relational entities; reject static substances

entries.name: Philosophy of Science
entries.core_principles: Nature of scientific knowledge; demarcation problem; theory confirmation; paradigms; scientific realism vs anti-realism
entries.application: Examine scientific method; theory evaluation; demarcate science from non-science; paradigm analysis; realism questions

entries.name: Philosophy of Language
entries.core_principles: Meaning, reference, communication; speech acts; linguistic context; semantics and pragmatics; language games
entries.application: Analyze meaning and reference; speech act theory; contextual semantics; pragmatic dimension; language use

entries.name: Philosophy of Mathematics
entries.core_principles: Nature of mathematical truth; platonism vs nominalism; foundations; mathematical ontology; logicism, intuitionism, formalism
entries.application: Examine mathematical foundations; ontology of numbers; truth nature; foundational programs; mathematical reasoning

entries.name: Bioethics
entries.core_principles: Medical ethics; autonomy, beneficence, non-maleficence, justice; life and death decisions; enhancement; research ethics
entries.application: Apply ethical principles to medicine; respect autonomy; beneficence and non-maleficence; just distribution; informed consent

entries.name: Environmental Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Human relationship to nature; intrinsic value of nature; deep ecology; land ethic; sustainability; ecological citizenship
entries.application: Recognize intrinsic natural value; ecological thinking; sustainability priority; land ethic; environmental responsibility

entries.name: Philosophy of Technology
entries.core_principles: Technology's impact on humanity; mediation; instrumental vs substantive views; technological determinism; ethics of technology
entries.application: Examine technological mediation; human-technology relation; technological shaping; ethical assessment; critical technology studies

entries.name: Transhumanism
entries.core_principles: Human enhancement through technology; overcoming biological limits; morphological freedom; longevity; cognitive enhancement
entries.application: Support technological enhancement; transcend biological limits; morphological freedom; life extension; intelligence augmentation

entries.name: Effective Altruism
entries.core_principles: Maximize good through reason and evidence; cause prioritization; expected value; earning to give; long-termism
entries.application: Evidence-based giving; cause prioritization; expected value calculation; maximize impact; rational altruism; long-term thinking

entries.name: Objectivism
entries.core_principles: Ayn Rand; rational self-interest; objective reality; reason as only source of knowledge; laissez-faire capitalism; individual rights
entries.application: Rational self-interest as virtue; objective reality; reason paramount; productive achievement; individual rights; free markets

entries.name: Cosmopolitanism
entries.core_principles: Global citizenship; universal moral community; shared humanity; transnational justice; world community; human rights
entries.application: Global perspective; universal human dignity; transnational obligations; world citizenship; shared humanity; global justice

entries.name: Data-Driven Decision Making
entries.core_principles: Base decisions on evidence and metrics rather than intuition alone
entries.application: Collect relevant data, analyze objectively, test assumptions, measure outcomes, iterate based on results

entries.name: Reversible vs Irreversible Decisions
entries.core_principles: Move fast on reversible decisions, deliberate carefully on irreversible ones
entries.application: Identify decision type, use appropriate speed and rigor, enable quick iteration on Type 2 decisions

entries.name: Disagree and Commit
entries.core_principles: Debate vigorously, then commit fully once decision is made
entries.application: Encourage dissent during discussion, demand alignment after decision, no undermining post-decision

entries.name: First Principles Thinking
entries.core_principles: Break problems down to fundamental truths and reason up from there
entries.application: Question assumptions, identify core truths, rebuild reasoning from foundation, avoid reasoning by analogy

entries.name: Expected Value Optimization
entries.core_principles: Maximize expected value considering both probability and magnitude
entries.application: Assess likelihood, estimate impact, calculate expected value, make high-EV bets even with risk

entries.name: Opportunity Cost Awareness
entries.core_principles: Consider what you're giving up by choosing one path over another
entries.application: Identify alternatives, compare true costs including forgone opportunities, choose highest value option

entries.name: Bias Toward Action
entries.core_principles: Default to doing rather than deliberating when uncertainty is high
entries.application: Start before feeling ready, learn through action, iterate quickly, avoid analysis paralysis

entries.name: Think in Bets
entries.core_principles: Frame decisions as bets with odds and stakes rather than binary right/wrong
entries.application: Acknowledge uncertainty, assign probabilities, size bets appropriately, update based on outcomes

entries.name: Regret Minimization Framework
entries.core_principles: Choose paths that minimize long-term regret
entries.application: Project forward to end of life, imagine looking back, identify what would cause regret, act accordingly

entries.name: Second-Order Thinking
entries.core_principles: Consider consequences of consequences, not just immediate effects
entries.application: Ask 'and then what?', trace multiple levels of impact, anticipate unintended consequences

entries.name: Customer Obsession
entries.core_principles: Start with customer needs and work backward
entries.application: Listen to customers deeply, solve real problems, measure customer satisfaction, prioritize customer value

entries.name: Jobs to be Done Mindset
entries.core_principles: Customers hire products to get jobs done in their lives
entries.application: Understand functional and emotional jobs, design solutions for jobs not features, measure job success

entries.name: Product-Market Fit Obsession
entries.core_principles: Nothing else matters until you achieve product-market fit
entries.application: Focus relentlessly on PMF, measure it rigorously, don't scale prematurely, iterate until fit is achieved

entries.name: Customer Lifetime Value Focus
entries.core_principles: Optimize for long-term customer value, not single transactions
entries.application: Invest in retention, measure LTV, balance acquisition and retention, build sustainable relationships

entries.name: Outcome Over Output
entries.core_principles: Measure customer outcomes achieved, not features shipped
entries.application: Define success by customer results, track outcome metrics, validate value delivery, iterate on impact

entries.name: Voice of Customer Integration
entries.core_principles: Systematically incorporate customer feedback into decisions
entries.application: Gather feedback continuously, analyze for patterns, close feedback loops, act on insights

entries.name: Customer Success Partnership
entries.core_principles: View customer relationships as true partnerships toward mutual success
entries.application: Align incentives, invest in customer success, share knowledge generously, grow together

entries.name: Segment-Specific Value
entries.core_principles: Different customer segments need different value propositions
entries.application: Segment thoughtfully, tailor offerings, customize messaging, serve each segment appropriately

entries.name: Customer Effort Reduction
entries.core_principles: Make it easy for customers to do business with you
entries.application: Reduce friction, simplify processes, enable self-service, remove unnecessary steps

entries.name: Proactive Value Delivery
entries.core_principles: Anticipate needs and deliver value before being asked
entries.application: Monitor customer health, identify opportunities early, reach out proactively, prevent issues

entries.name: Move Fast and Break Things
entries.core_principles: Prioritize speed and learning over perfection in early stages
entries.application: Ship quickly, accept some breakage, learn from failures, iterate rapidly, optimize for learning speed

entries.name: Ship to Learn
entries.core_principles: The fastest way to learn is to ship and gather real feedback
entries.application: Release early versions, get real usage data, learn from customers, iterate based on reality

entries.name: 80/20 Execution
entries.core_principles: Focus on the 20% of effort that drives 80% of results
entries.application: Identify high-leverage activities, deprioritize low-impact work, maximize output per input

entries.name: Done is Better Than Perfect
entries.core_principles: Shipping imperfect work beats endlessly polishing
entries.application: Set 'good enough' thresholds, avoid perfectionism, release and iterate, value momentum

entries.name: Bias for Simplicity
entries.core_principles: Choose simple solutions over complex ones when possible
entries.application: Question complexity, remove unnecessary elements, make things obvious, reduce cognitive load

entries.name: Ruthless Prioritization
entries.core_principles: Say no to good ideas to focus on great ones
entries.application: Limit WIP, focus intensely, kill projects, protect focus, choose few bets

entries.name: Compound Progress
entries.core_principles: Small consistent improvements compound into massive gains
entries.application: Improve 1% daily, build habits, track progress, celebrate small wins, trust compounding

entries.name: Parallel Work Streams
entries.core_principles: Execute multiple initiatives concurrently when dependencies allow
entries.application: Identify independent workstreams, parallelize execution, coordinate effectively, maximize throughput

entries.name: Timebox Exploration
entries.core_principles: Set time limits on investigation and experimentation
entries.application: Define exploration windows, commit to decision deadlines, act on time even with uncertainty

entries.name: Momentum Preservation
entries.core_principles: Maintain forward motion even when optimal path is unclear
entries.application: Keep shipping, maintain cadence, avoid stalls, course-correct while moving

entries.name: Growth Mindset
entries.core_principles: Abilities can be developed through dedication and effort
entries.application: Embrace challenges, persist through obstacles, see effort as path to mastery, learn from criticism

entries.name: Experimentation Culture
entries.core_principles: Run experiments continuously to discover what works
entries.application: Form hypotheses, design tests, measure results, scale winners, kill losers quickly

entries.name: Fail Fast, Learn Faster
entries.core_principles: Quick failures are valuable learning opportunities
entries.application: Test quickly, identify failures early, extract lessons, apply learnings, move on

entries.name: 10x Thinking
entries.core_principles: Aim for 10x improvement, not 10% - it forces different approaches
entries.application: Set ambitious goals, question constraints, reimagine solutions, challenge incremental thinking

entries.name: Beginner's Mind
entries.core_principles: Approach problems without preconceptions, as a beginner would
entries.application: Question assumptions, see with fresh eyes, avoid expertise bias, stay curious

entries.name: Adjacent Possible Exploration
entries.core_principles: Explore opportunities just beyond current capabilities
entries.application: Map current state, identify adjacent opportunities, take calculated leaps, expand gradually

entries.name: Combinatorial Innovation
entries.core_principles: Combine existing ideas in novel ways to create innovation
entries.application: Study diverse fields, find unexpected connections, recombine concepts, cross-pollinate ideas

entries.name: Constraint-Driven Innovation
entries.core_principles: Constraints force creative solutions
entries.application: Embrace limitations, design around constraints, find creative workarounds, use scarcity productively

entries.name: Build-Measure-Learn
entries.core_principles: Iterate through rapid cycles of building, measuring, and learning
entries.application: Build minimal versions, measure real usage, learn from data, iterate quickly

entries.name: Question Core Assumptions
entries.core_principles: Regularly challenge fundamental beliefs about your business
entries.application: List key assumptions, test them explicitly, update beliefs, pivot when warranted

entries.name: Radical Transparency
entries.core_principles: Share information openly by default, restrict only when necessary
entries.application: Default to public, share metrics widely, communicate openly, build trust through transparency

entries.name: High Trust, Low Process
entries.core_principles: Trust people to do the right thing rather than adding process
entries.application: Hire well, trust judgment, minimize bureaucracy, add process only when needed

entries.name: Ownership Mentality
entries.core_principles: Act like owners, not employees - take initiative and responsibility
entries.application: Empower decision-making, reward ownership, hold accountable, think long-term

entries.name: No Ego, Best Idea Wins
entries.core_principles: Ideas are judged on merit, not who proposes them
entries.application: Encourage all voices, debate ideas vigorously, check egos, implement best solutions

entries.name: Psychological Safety
entries.core_principles: Create environment where people feel safe to take risks and be vulnerable
entries.application: Welcome questions, reward speaking up, punish silence on problems, model vulnerability

entries.name: Extreme Candor
entries.core_principles: Care personally and challenge directly
entries.application: Give honest feedback, show you care, address issues directly, build genuine relationships

entries.name: Written Communication
entries.core_principles: Document decisions and thinking in writing
entries.application: Write memos, document decisions, think through writing, create shared understanding

entries.name: Hire for Slope, Not Y-Intercept
entries.core_principles: Hire for learning rate and potential, not just current skills
entries.application: Assess learning ability, value trajectory, invest in development, grow talent

entries.name: Team Over Individual
entries.core_principles: Optimize for team success, not individual heroics
entries.application: Reward collaboration, share credit, support teammates, build collective capability

entries.name: Default to Action
entries.core_principles: When in doubt, do something rather than nothing
entries.application: Bias toward trying, accept some mistakes, learn by doing, avoid overthinking

entries.name: Capital Efficiency
entries.core_principles: Maximize output per dollar of capital deployed
entries.application: Measure capital productivity, optimize spend, demonstrate ROI, build lean operations

entries.name: Invest in Leverage
entries.core_principles: Allocate resources to activities with multiplier effects
entries.application: Identify high-leverage work, invest in force multipliers, build scalable assets

entries.name: Bet on Strengths
entries.core_principles: Double down on what works rather than fixing weaknesses
entries.application: Identify core strengths, invest heavily there, partner or outsource weaknesses

entries.name: Portfolio Approach
entries.core_principles: Balance portfolio of safe bets and high-risk/high-reward initiatives
entries.application: Diversify investments, manage risk consciously, balance core and exploration

entries.name: Zero-Based Resource Allocation
entries.core_principles: Justify all resource allocation from scratch, not historically
entries.application: Question existing allocations, reallocate based on current priorities, avoid inertia

entries.name: Focus Over Coverage
entries.core_principles: Win decisively in chosen areas rather than being mediocre everywhere
entries.application: Choose battles, concentrate resources, dominate niches, avoid spreading thin

entries.name: Time as Scarce Resource
entries.core_principles: Treat time as the ultimate non-renewable resource
entries.application: Guard time fiercely, prioritize ruthlessly, eliminate time wasters, value speed

entries.name: Strategic Patience
entries.core_principles: Be patient for growth, impatient for profit/learning
entries.application: Allow compound growth time, demand quick learning cycles, balance urgency and patience

entries.name: Build vs Buy vs Partner
entries.core_principles: Deliberately choose between building, buying, or partnering for capabilities
entries.application: Assess strategic value, evaluate options, choose appropriate path, move decisively

entries.name: Opportunity Fund Reserves
entries.core_principles: Maintain capacity to seize unexpected opportunities
entries.application: Keep resources in reserve, preserve optionality, move quickly on opportunities

entries.name: Differentiate or Die
entries.core_principles: Clear differentiation is essential for survival
entries.application: Define unique value, avoid commoditization, build defensibility, communicate difference

entries.name: Network Effects Obsession
entries.core_principles: Build products that get better as more people use them
entries.application: Design for network effects, achieve critical mass, defend against competitors, compound advantage

entries.name: Winner-Take-Most Markets
entries.core_principles: In many markets, #1 captures disproportionate value
entries.application: Race to dominance, invest aggressively in leadership, avoid being #3 or worse

entries.name: Category Creation
entries.core_principles: Create and own new categories rather than competing in existing ones
entries.application: Define new category, educate market, set standards, become category leader

entries.name: Compete on Different Dimensions
entries.core_principles: Win by changing the game, not playing the same game better
entries.application: Identify new value dimensions, shift basis of competition, make current game irrelevant

entries.name: Strategic Judo
entries.core_principles: Use competitors' size and strength against them
entries.application: Identify incumbent weaknesses, exploit structural disadvantages, move where they can't follow

entries.name: Fast Follower Strategy
entries.core_principles: Learn from pioneers' mistakes and execute better
entries.application: Let others validate market, observe what works, execute with improvements, move fast

entries.name: Moat Building
entries.core_principles: Continuously strengthen competitive advantages and barriers
entries.application: Identify moat types, invest in defensibility, widen moat over time, protect advantages

entries.name: Asymmetric Warfare
entries.core_principles: Fight battles where you have unfair advantages
entries.application: Choose favorable battlegrounds, leverage unique strengths, avoid direct strength-on-strength competition

entries.name: Coopetition Mindset
entries.core_principles: Sometimes cooperating with competitors creates more value than competing
entries.application: Identify win-win scenarios, build standards together, compete and cooperate strategically

entries.name: Asymmetric Risk-Taking
entries.core_principles: Take risks with limited downside and unlimited upside
entries.application: Seek convex payoffs, limit losses, preserve optionality, bet where you can't lose much but can win big

entries.name: Antifragility
entries.core_principles: Build systems that gain from volatility and disorder
entries.application: Design for resilience, benefit from shocks, avoid fragility, get stronger from stress

entries.name: Margin of Safety
entries.core_principles: Build in buffer for uncertainty and unknowns
entries.application: Plan conservatively, expect surprises, maintain reserves, avoid fragile dependencies

entries.name: Pre-Mortem Thinking
entries.core_principles: Imagine failure and work backward to identify risks
entries.application: Assume failure, identify causes, mitigate risks proactively, plan contingencies

entries.name: Redundancy Over Efficiency
entries.core_principles: Accept some inefficiency for resilience and reliability
entries.application: Build backup systems, maintain spare capacity, avoid single points of failure

entries.name: Informed Risk-Taking
entries.core_principles: Take calculated risks based on understanding, not blind bets
entries.application: Assess risks explicitly, understand probability and impact, choose consciously

entries.name: Tail Risk Awareness
entries.core_principles: Pay attention to low-probability, high-impact events
entries.application: Identify tail risks, protect against ruin, don't ignore unlikely disasters

entries.name: Barbell Strategy
entries.core_principles: Combine extreme safety with extreme risk, avoid middle ground
entries.application: Protect core with conservative approach, take aggressive risks with small portion

entries.name: Kill Your Darlings
entries.core_principles: Be willing to abandon even beloved projects when they're not working
entries.application: Assess objectively, cut losses quickly, avoid sunk cost fallacy, reallocate resources

entries.name: Prepare for Multiple Scenarios
entries.core_principles: Plan for various futures rather than betting on one
entries.application: Develop scenarios, prepare contingencies, maintain flexibility, adapt to reality

entries.name: Create Value, Then Capture
entries.core_principles: Focus first on creating real value, then on capturing fair share
entries.application: Solve real problems, deliver genuine value, build pricing power through value creation

entries.name: Win-Win Philosophy
entries.core_principles: Structure interactions for mutual benefit when possible
entries.application: Seek positive-sum outcomes, align incentives, build long-term partnerships

entries.name: Value-Based Pricing
entries.core_principles: Price based on value delivered, not costs incurred
entries.application: Understand customer value, price to value, justify premium pricing, capture fair share

entries.name: Stakeholder Balance
entries.core_principles: Create value for all stakeholders, not just shareholders
entries.application: Consider customers, employees, partners, community; balance interests, build sustainability

entries.name: Continuous Value Addition
entries.core_principles: Keep adding value to maintain and grow relationships
entries.application: Never rest on past value, innovate continuously, deepen relationships, prevent commoditization

entries.name: Economic Value Creation
entries.core_principles: Generate returns above cost of capital sustainably
entries.application: Measure economic profit, optimize ROIC, create real economic value, sustain over time

entries.name: Platform Value Creation
entries.core_principles: Create value by connecting and enabling others
entries.application: Build two-sided markets, enable transactions, take fair platform fee, grow ecosystem

entries.name: Leverage Asymmetry
entries.core_principles: Create situations where you benefit more than you cost
entries.application: Find asymmetric opportunities, scale value delivery, capture increasing returns

entries.name: Compounding Value
entries.core_principles: Build value that compounds over time
entries.application: Make cumulative improvements, benefit from compound effects, think in decades

entries.name: Pricing Power Focus
entries.core_principles: Build businesses that can raise prices without losing customers
entries.application: Create differentiation, build switching costs, demonstrate unique value, avoid commoditization

entries.name: Measure What Matters
entries.core_principles: Track metrics that actually drive business outcomes
entries.application: Define key metrics, avoid vanity metrics, measure leading indicators, act on data

entries.name: Systems Over Goals
entries.core_principles: Build systems and processes that naturally produce desired outcomes
entries.application: Design good systems, trust process, measure system health, improve continuously

entries.name: Automate and Eliminate
entries.core_principles: Automate repeatable work, eliminate unnecessary work entirely
entries.application: Identify repetitive tasks, automate where possible, question necessity, eliminate waste

entries.name: Direct Responsibility
entries.core_principles: Every important outcome has one person directly responsible
entries.application: Assign clear ownership, avoid diffused responsibility, hold accountable, empower owners

entries.name: Blameless Post-Mortems
entries.core_principles: Learn from failures without blame to encourage honesty
entries.application: Focus on system improvements, avoid punishment, extract lessons, prevent recurrence

entries.name: Working Backwards
entries.core_principles: Start from desired customer experience and work backward to requirements
entries.application: Write press release first, define customer experience, determine requirements, build to spec

entries.name: Single-Threaded Leadership
entries.core_principles: Important initiatives deserve dedicated leadership focus
entries.application: Assign dedicated leaders, avoid split attention on critical projects, resource appropriately

entries.name: Two-Pizza Teams
entries.core_principles: Keep teams small enough to feed with two pizzas (6-10 people)
entries.application: Limit team size, maintain agility, reduce communication overhead, empower small teams

entries.name: Day One Mentality
entries.core_principles: Maintain startup mindset and urgency regardless of company size
entries.application: Resist complacency, stay hungry, move fast, question bureaucracy, maintain scrappiness

entries.name: Think Long-Term
entries.core_principles: Optimize for long-term value over short-term gains
entries.application: Make multi-year bets, resist quarterly pressure, invest in future, build durable advantages

entries.name: Customer Value is Paramount

entries.essence: Prioritize building lasting customer relationships over one-time sales

entries.name: Listen to Customer Feedback
entries.essence: Customer input is invaluable for product and service improvement

entries.essence: Trust is earned through consistent delivery and integrity

entries.name: Delight, Don't Just Satisfy
entries.essence: Exceeding expectations creates memorable experiences and loyalty

entries.essence: Keeping existing customers is more cost-effective than finding new ones

entries.essence: Reduce friction and complexity in all customer interactions

entries.essence: Ethical behavior and honesty are fundamental requirements, not options

entries.essence: Open, honest communication strengthens all relationships

entries.essence: Deliver on promises and own up to failures

entries.essence: Acknowledge errors promptly and take corrective action

entries.essence: Balance interests of customers, employees, investors, and community

entries.essence: Legal compliance is baseline; ethical behavior goes beyond

entries.essence: Customer privacy and data security are sacred responsibilities

entries.essence: Encourage reporting of issues without fear of punishment

entries.name: Reputation Takes Years to Build, Moments to Destroy
entries.essence: Protect reputation through consistent ethical behavior

entries.name: Execution Beats Ideas
entries.essence: Great execution of good ideas trumps poor execution of great ideas

entries.name: Speed Matters
entries.essence: Velocity creates competitive advantage and learning opportunities

entries.name: Done is Better Than Perfect
entries.essence: Shipping imperfect work enables iteration and learning

entries.name: Measure What Matters
entries.essence: Track metrics that actually drive business outcomes

entries.name: Focus is a Competitive Advantage
entries.essence: Doing few things well beats doing many things poorly

entries.name: Quality Compounds
entries.essence: Consistent quality builds reputation and customer loyalty over time

entries.name: Bias Toward Action
entries.essence: When uncertain, take action and learn rather than endlessly deliberating

entries.name: Simplicity Scales
entries.essence: Simple solutions are easier to execute, maintain, and scale

entries.essence: Clear ownership and responsibility produce better outcomes

entries.name: Diversity Strengthens Teams
entries.essence: Diverse perspectives lead to better decisions and innovation

entries.essence: Pay people fairly based on the value they create

entries.name: Eliminate Waste
entries.essence: Non-value-adding activities drain resources and slow progress

entries.name: Continuous Improvement
entries.essence: Small, consistent improvements compound into major gains

entries.name: Standardize to Scale
entries.essence: Standardization enables efficient scaling and quality control

entries.name: Optimize the Whole, Not Parts
entries.essence: System-level optimization beats local optimization

entries.name: Build Redundancy for Critical Functions
entries.essence: Accept some inefficiency to ensure reliability and resilience

entries.name: Automate Repetitive Work
entries.essence: Free people for high-value work by automating routine tasks

entries.name: Plan for Scale
entries.essence: Design operations to handle 10x growth from day one

entries.name: Fix Root Causes
entries.essence: Address underlying issues, not just symptoms

entries.name: Make Reversible Decisions Quickly
entries.essence: Speed matters more than perfection on decisions that can be undone

entries.name: Data Informs, Judgment Decides
entries.essence: Use data to inform decisions, but don't abdicate judgment to metrics

entries.name: Disagree and Commit
entries.essence: Once decisions are made, everyone commits fully regardless of initial position

entries.name: Seek Diverse Perspectives
entries.essence: Better decisions emerge from considering multiple viewpoints

entries.name: Consider Second-Order Effects
entries.essence: Think through consequences of consequences, not just immediate impacts

entries.name: Acknowledge Uncertainty
entries.essence: Be explicit about what is known, unknown, and unknowable

entries.name: Optimize for Expected Value
entries.essence: Make decisions that maximize expected outcomes over time

entries.name: Learn from Outcomes
entries.essence: Review decision outcomes to improve future decision-making

entries.name: Decide at the Right Level
entries.essence: Push decisions to those closest to information and impact

entries.name: Act on Conviction, Not Consensus
entries.essence: Seek input widely, but don't require unanimous agreement to act

block.label: LangChain Runtime Subject
block.text: The subject is LangChain/LangGraph execution as an optional runtime bridge for model, tool, retrieval, and graph workflows.

title: use.business.library.v1.0.0
reason: unsupported role use preserved in legacy only

legacySource.description: Business-domain MOLT Use blocks. Use answers: 'What is this for?' Each block describes the business function or purpose of the Subject — what that thing does or enables in a commercial context.
legacySource.usage: Select the Use block that best describes what the business Subject is being used for. Use anchors the reasoning chain between Subject and Aim in business contexts.

SALES_REVENUE.description: Uses related to generating and closing revenue

blocks.function: To identify and attract potential buyers into the sales funnel
examples: cold outreach campaign
inbound content lead capture
trade show contact collection

blocks.function: To determine which leads have genuine buying potential and fit
examples: BANT assessment
discovery call scoring
ICP matching

blocks.function: To advance an opportunity through defined pipeline stages toward close
examples: moving a deal from proposal to negotiation
advancing a prospect past security review
progressing a multi-stakeholder deal

blocks.function: To obtain a formal commitment and signed agreement from the buyer
examples: presenting final commercial terms
executing a contract
securing a purchase order

blocks.function: To increase revenue from existing customers by expanding scope, seats, or tiers
examples: offering a premium tier upgrade
adding additional user licenses
expanding a contract to new business units

MARKETING_DEMAND.description: Uses related to building awareness, demand, and brand

blocks.function: To create and reinforce a consistent identity and reputation in the market
examples: brand campaign execution
thought leadership publishing
sponsorship and event presence

blocks.function: To create market interest that produces a pipeline of qualified opportunities
examples: running a paid media campaign
hosting a webinar series
publishing high-intent SEO content

blocks.function: To position an offering against alternatives and communicate its value to target buyers
examples: writing a competitive battle card
creating a product one-pager
developing pricing and packaging messaging

blocks.function: To deploy coordinated marketing activities across channels to a defined audience
examples: running an email nurture sequence
executing a multi-channel product launch
managing a retargeting campaign

blocks.function: To keep existing customers engaged and reduce churn through ongoing communication
examples: sending a customer success newsletter
running a loyalty rewards program
providing early renewal incentives

title: use.coding.library.v1.0.0
reason: unsupported role use preserved in legacy only

legacySource.description: Coding-domain MOLT Use blocks. Use answers: 'What is this for?' Each block describes the software engineering function or purpose of the Subject — what that code, tool, pattern, or construct does or enables.
legacySource.usage: Select the Use block that best describes what the coding Subject is being used for. Use anchors the reasoning chain between Subject and Aim in software engineering contexts.

CODE_AUTHORING.description: Uses related to writing and structuring code

blocks.function: To write new code that delivers a defined user-facing or system capability
examples: implementing a login form
adding a REST endpoint
building a file upload handler

blocks.function: To modify existing code to correct unintended behavior
examples: patching a null pointer exception
fixing an off-by-one error
correcting an incorrect regex match

blocks.function: To restructure existing code to improve quality without changing behavior
examples: extracting a repeated block into a function
renaming variables for clarity
replacing a deeply nested conditional with early returns

blocks.function: To produce repetitive scaffolding code that follows a known pattern
examples: generating a CRUD controller
scaffolding a React component
creating a migration file

blocks.function: To write code that executes a specific computational procedure
examples: implementing binary search
coding a graph traversal
writing a sorting routine

CODE_REVIEW.description: Uses related to evaluating and improving code written by others

blocks.function: To check whether code does what it claims and handles edge cases
examples: verifying a function returns correct output
checking boundary conditions
confirming error paths are handled

blocks.function: To ensure code follows team or language standards for readability
examples: checking naming conventions
enforcing line length limits
verifying consistent use of async/await vs. promises

blocks.function: To identify vulnerabilities and risky patterns in submitted code
examples: checking for SQL injection vectors
reviewing input sanitization
flagging hardcoded secrets

blocks.function: To identify code patterns that will cause slowness or excessive resource use at scale
examples: spotting N+1 query patterns
flagging synchronous operations on hot paths
identifying unindexed database lookups

blocks.function: To evaluate whether code fits the intended structural design of the system
examples: checking layer separation is respected
verifying domain logic is not leaking into controllers
confirming a new service follows the established pattern

DEBUGGING.description: Uses related to diagnosing and resolving failures in running code

blocks.function: To follow a stack trace or error message back to its origin
examples: reading a Python traceback
parsing a Java NullPointerException stack
tracing a segfault to the offending call

blocks.function: To examine the values of variables and data structures at a point in execution
examples: adding print statements to log state
using a debugger to inspect heap objects
dumping a request payload to find a malformed field

blocks.function: To trigger a bug reliably in a controlled environment before fixing it
examples: writing a minimal repro case
adding a failing test that captures the bug
isolating the input that triggers the failure

blocks.function: To narrow down which component, function, or line is responsible for a failure
examples: bisecting a git history to find a regression
disabling components one by one to isolate a fault
adding assertions to narrow the fault to a single function

blocks.function: To extract diagnostic information from runtime output to understand system behavior
examples: searching logs for an error pattern
correlating timestamps across services
parsing structured log fields to trace a request

TESTING.description: Uses related to verifying code correctness through automated and manual tests

blocks.function: To verify that a single function or class behaves correctly in isolation
examples: asserting a function returns expected output
testing a class method with mocked dependencies
checking boundary values for a validator

blocks.function: To verify that components interact correctly when connected
examples: testing an API handler against a real database
verifying a service-to-service call works end to end
checking that a message producer and consumer interact correctly

blocks.function: To simulate real user flows through the full system stack
examples: Playwright test of a checkout flow
Cypress test of a login and navigation sequence
automated browser test of a form submission

blocks.function: To confirm that a change has not broken previously working behavior
examples: re-running the full test suite after a refactor
snapshot testing a UI component after a style change
comparing API response shapes before and after a migration

blocks.function: To verify code against automatically generated inputs that cover many cases
examples: using Hypothesis to test a parser
fuzzing a serializer with random inputs
property testing a sort function with random arrays

DOCUMENTATION.description: Uses related to explaining code to current and future developers

blocks.function: To explain code intent and logic directly alongside the source code
examples: writing a docstring for a function
adding a comment explaining a non-obvious algorithm
documenting a complex regex pattern

blocks.function: To describe how an interface should be called, what it expects, and what it returns
examples: writing OpenAPI spec for a REST endpoint
documenting function signature and return type
writing a README section on API usage

blocks.function: To describe the high-level structure, design decisions, and component relationships of a system
examples: writing an ADR for a technology choice
documenting a service dependency diagram
explaining the data flow through a pipeline

blocks.function: To help a new developer understand a codebase and start contributing quickly
examples: writing a CONTRIBUTING.md
documenting local development setup steps
creating a glossary of domain terms used in the code

blocks.function: To record what changed between versions in a format useful to consumers
examples: writing a CHANGELOG.md entry
documenting breaking changes for an API version
summarizing a release in a GitHub release note

DEPENDENCY_MANAGEMENT.description: Uses related to managing external libraries and packages

blocks.function: To add a third-party library to the project
examples: running npm install lodash
adding a pip dependency
adding a Cargo crate to Cargo.toml

blocks.function: To check dependencies for known vulnerabilities, license conflicts, or stale versions
examples: running npm audit
checking for CVEs in pip packages
reviewing dependency licenses for GPL conflicts

blocks.function: To lock a dependency to a specific version to ensure reproducible builds
examples: pinning a package in requirements.txt
using a lockfile to freeze versions
specifying exact version constraints in go.mod

blocks.function: To update a library to a newer version to gain fixes, features, or security patches
examples: upgrading a React version
bumping a vulnerable package
migrating to a new major version of an SDK

blocks.function: To eliminate an unused or problematic dependency from the project
examples: removing an unmaintained library
eliminating a transitive dependency with a CVE
replacing a heavy package with a lightweight custom implementation

BUILD_TOOLING.description: Uses related to compiling, bundling, and preparing code for execution

blocks.function: To translate source code into a runnable or deployable artifact
examples: compiling TypeScript to JavaScript
building a Go binary
compiling Rust to WASM

blocks.function: To combine multiple source files into a single optimized output

using Vite to bundle a frontend
rollup bundling a library for distribution

blocks.function: To convert code from one language or syntax to a compatible but different form
examples: Babel transpiling ES2023 to ES5
converting TypeScript to JavaScript
transpiling modern CSS for older browsers

blocks.function: To reduce code size for production delivery by removing whitespace and renaming symbols
examples: minifying a JavaScript bundle
compressing a CSS file
tree-shaking unused imports from a build

blocks.function: To package compiled code into a distributable format
examples: building a Docker image
creating an npm package tarball
packaging a Python wheel

VERSION_CONTROL.description: Uses related to managing code history and collaboration via git or similar

blocks.function: To record a meaningful, atomic unit of change with a clear message
examples: writing a conventional commit message
staging and committing a bug fix
squashing work-in-progress commits before merge

blocks.function: To create and manage isolated lines of development
examples: creating a feature branch
naming branches by convention
pruning stale remote branches

blocks.function: To integrate changes from one branch into another
examples: rebasing a feature branch onto main
resolving a merge conflict
fast-forward merging a hotfix

blocks.function: To examine past changes to understand when, why, and by whom something was changed
examples: running git blame to trace a line
using git log to find a regression commit
reviewing diff history for a file

blocks.function: To mark a specific commit as a named version for release or reference
examples: creating a semantic version tag
drafting a GitHub release from a tag
tagging a known-good commit for rollback reference

CODE_GENERATION.description: Uses related to automatically producing code from templates, specs, or models

blocks.function: To generate a starter project or component structure from a template
examples: running create-react-app
using a Yeoman generator
running rails new to scaffold an app

blocks.function: To produce code from a data or API schema definition
examples: generating TypeScript types from an OpenAPI spec
generating gRPC stubs from a proto file
generating ORM models from a database schema

blocks.function: To automatically produce test cases from source code or specifications
examples: generating unit test stubs from a class
generating property tests from a type signature
producing test fixtures from example data

blocks.function: To produce database migration scripts from a schema diff
examples: generating an Alembic migration from model changes
auto-generating a Rails migration
producing a Prisma migration from schema changes

blocks.function: To produce SDK or client code from an API specification
examples: generating a Python SDK from OpenAPI
producing a JavaScript client from a GraphQL schema
generating a gRPC client stub

PERFORMANCE_OPTIMISATION.description: Uses related to making code faster or more resource-efficient

blocks.function: To measure where a program spends time or memory during execution
examples: running a CPU profile on a slow endpoint
using a memory profiler to find a leak
profiling database query execution plans

blocks.function: To store computed results for reuse so expensive operations are not repeated
examples: adding an in-memory cache for a database query
caching an API response in Redis
memoizing a recursive function

blocks.function: To improve the efficiency of database queries
examples: adding an index to a slow query
rewriting an N+1 query as a join
using a covering index to avoid a table scan

blocks.function: To run multiple tasks in parallel or overlap I/O-bound work to improve throughput
examples: using async/await to overlap API calls
parallelising a data processing pipeline
using a thread pool for CPU-bound work

blocks.function: To defer loading or computing a resource until it is actually needed
examples: lazy importing a heavy module
loading images only when in viewport
deferring a database join until fields are accessed

SECURITY_HARDENING.description: Uses related to making code safer against attacks and misuse

blocks.function: To reject or sanitize untrusted input before it is processed
examples: validating a form field against a schema
rejecting unexpected characters in a URL parameter
stripping HTML tags from user-supplied content

blocks.function: To verify identity before allowing access to protected resources
examples: checking a JWT before serving an endpoint
verifying an API key on each request
enforcing session validity before a sensitive action

blocks.function: To confirm the authenticated user has permission for the requested action
examples: checking a user's role before a write operation
verifying resource ownership before an update
enforcing scope restrictions on an OAuth token

blocks.function: To handle credentials, keys, and tokens securely in code and environments
examples: reading secrets from environment variables rather than hardcoding
rotating an API key without downtime
using a vault to inject secrets at runtime

blocks.function: To eliminate known security flaws in third-party packages
examples: upgrading a package with a published CVE
replacing a vulnerable transitive dependency
applying a security advisory patch

API_DESIGN.description: Uses related to designing interfaces between systems or components

blocks.function: To define the shape, verbs, and semantics of an HTTP or RPC interface
examples: designing REST resource routes
defining a GraphQL query and mutation schema
specifying gRPC service methods

blocks.function: To define the data structures exchanged at an API boundary
examples: writing an OpenAPI schema for a request body
defining a proto message for a service call
specifying a GraphQL type for a response

blocks.function: To define how failures are communicated across an API boundary
examples: designing HTTP error response shapes
defining gRPC status codes for service errors
specifying error envelopes in a JSON API

blocks.function: To plan how the API will evolve without breaking existing consumers
examples: designing URL path versioning
using Accept header versioning
planning a field deprecation strategy

blocks.function: To define how the API constrains request volume to protect the system
examples: designing per-token rate limits
specifying retry-after header semantics
planning burst vs. sustained rate limits

DATA_MODELLING.description: Uses related to designing how data is structured and stored

blocks.function: To define the structure of data stored in a database or passed through a system
examples: designing a relational table schema
defining a MongoDB document shape
specifying a Kafka message schema

blocks.function: To restructure data to eliminate redundancy and improve consistency
examples: extracting a repeated value into a lookup table
splitting a wide table into related tables
removing derived fields that can be computed

blocks.function: To write the changes needed to evolve a database schema safely
examples: writing a migration to add a nullable column
authoring a migration to rename a table
writing a data backfill migration

blocks.function: To define which columns or fields should be indexed to support query patterns
examples: adding a composite index for a common filter
deciding between B-tree and GIN index types
planning index coverage for a query plan

blocks.function: To convert data structures to and from a transmittable format
examples: serialising a Python object to JSON
encoding a message as Protobuf

neoblock.name: LangChain Error Recovery
neoblock.description: Normalize tool, provider, graph, and retrieval errors into explicit UMG warnings/errors without silent fallback.

ordered_steps.notes: Return structured error plus trace event. Fallback is allowed only when declared.

neoblock.name: LangChain LangGraph Orchestrator
neoblock.description: Use LangGraph for stateful, durable, approval-aware workflows when selected by the sleeve.

ordered_steps.notes: Choose graph path for multi-step, stateful, long-running, or approval-gated workflows.
Return graph state and final result in UMG-compatible format.

neoblock.name: LangChain MCP Adapter
neoblock.description: Load tools, resources, or prompts from MCP servers when the sleeve enables MCP bridge mode.

ordered_steps.notes: Load tools from declared MCP servers only.
Permission-filter discovered MCP tools before exposure.

neoblock.name: LangChain OpenClaw Tool Bridge
neoblock.description: Route LangChain tool calls into OpenClaw plugin/tool invocations and return normalized tool results.

ordered_steps.notes: Wrap OpenClaw tool declarations with safe invoke payloads.
Normalize tool result into UMG trace/result surface.

neoblock.name: LangChain Permission Gate
neoblock.description: Filter requested tools and action modes before model/tool registration.

ordered_steps.notes: Allow, deny, or checkpoint tools based on permission_level and risk_class.

neoblock.name: LangChain Provider Adapter
neoblock.description: Select static, dynamic, or local model provider configuration for LangChain execution.

ordered_steps.notes: Provider selection remains inside sleeve-declared provider budget and fallback rules.

neoblock.name: LangChain RAG Retrieval Adapter
neoblock.description: Attach retrieval, vector store, or external knowledge-base context to the active task.

ordered_steps.notes: Select 2-step, agentic, or hybrid retrieval based on sleeve mode.
Return retrieved chunks, source metadata, and confidence notes.

neoblock.name: LangChain Runtime Intake
neoblock.description: Normalize a UMG sleeve request into a LangChain Bridge call payload.

ordered_steps.notes: Normalize task, sleeve_id, runtime mode, provider, tools, retrieval, and trace settings.

neoblock.name: LangChain Structured Output Adapter
neoblock.description: Constrain the final result to a sleeve-declared JSON/object schema for safe downstream processing.

ordered_steps.notes: Parse and validate final result against runtime.contract structured_output schema.

neoblock.name: LangChain Tool Registry
neoblock.description: Convert approved UMG/OpenClaw tool declarations into LangChain-compatible tool surfaces.

ordered_steps.notes: Validate tool_id, tool_name, schemas, permission, risk, and runtime target.
Register only tools emitted by the permission gate.

neoblock.name: LangChain Trace Observability
neoblock.description: Map LangChain/LangGraph/tool events into UMG RuntimeSpec Trace events.

ordered_steps.notes: Collect provider selection, tool decisions, tool calls, retrieved context, approval checkpoints, errors, and final result.
