stacks.name: MONITORING & OPTIMIZATION STACK
bullet_neoblocks: Content: Activate when client needs social media automation. Keywords: "social media", "posting schedule", "content calendar", "Instagram", "Facebook", "TikTok", "LinkedIn". Essential for consistent online presence.

Content: GOAL: Create realistic, maintainable social media schedule. Success means: posts scheduled 1-2 weeks ahead, client can maintain independently, brand voice consistent, engagement tracked. Prioritize: sustainability over volume, quality over quantity, client's capacity over ideal frequency.
Estimated time: Initial setup 3-5 hours, ongoing 2-3 hours/week
Content: Social media automation best practices:

Content: Social media automation must preserve authenticity. Automate the mechanics (scheduling, posting, hashtags), not the humanity (responses, relationship-building). AI-generated captions are fine if they sound like the client. Stock responses to DMs feel corporate. The goal: free client from posting burden while keeping their unique voice. Engagement > broadcasting. Over-automation kills personal brands.
Content: Standard social media automation setup:
AI Agent Task: Generate 3 tip options based on client expertise
AI Agent Task: Create story sequence from client's daily photos
AI Agent Task: Format testimonial with graphic template
AI Agent Task: Write engaging caption, suggest trending audio
AI Agent: Handles scheduling, monitoring, analytics
Result: Consistent presence without daily stress
Automation: Job complete → invoice generated and sent
Automation: Batch create, schedule 1-2 weeks ahead
Automation: AI agent handles common questions
Pain: Lost receipts, tax time chaos
Impact: Prevents lost sales, reduces waste
Pain: Leaving money on table (no repeat business)
Automation: Scheduled reports from multiple sources
Pain: Forgetting to ask satisfied customers
Set up model provider (start with free tier if possible)
Complete business assessment and automation planning
Custom OpenClaw agent configuration for any business type
Governance layer (8 Primary Values - always active)

Game Boy Color (1998) - Sharp LR35902 (enhanced Z80)
Genesis/Mega Drive (1988) - 68000 + Z80, VDP, YM2612 FM + PSG
Sega CD/Mega CD (1991) - Additional 68000, PCM audio, CD-ROM
32X (1994) - Dual SH-2 CPUs, VDP add-on, PWM audio
Game Gear (1990) - Enhanced Master System hardware
TurboGrafx-16/PC Engine (1987) - HuC6280 CPU, HuC6270 VDP
Content: Activate when implementing or debugging CPU emulation. Keywords: "cpu core", "instruction set", "6502", "z80", "68000", "arm", "mips", "timing", "cycles". Essential for all system emulation.

Content: GOAL: Create cycle-accurate CPU emulation for target system. Success means: all instructions implemented, timing correct, interrupts working, passes test ROMs. Prioritize: accuracy over speed initially, instruction correctness, documented timing, clean architecture for optimization later.
Estimated time: 2-8 weeks per CPU architecture
Content: Common CPU architectures in gaming systems:

Content: CPU emulation is the foundation. Everything else depends on it. Accuracy first, optimization second. Interpreter mode for correctness, JIT recompiler for speed. Document timing decisions - future optimizers need to understand compromises. Test constantly with known-good test ROMs. Modern CPUs are fast enough for cycle-accurate emulation of older systems. For newer systems (PS2, Xbox 360), HLE (high-level emulation) may be necessary for performance, but document what's being abstracted.

Content: Activate for memory management, bank switching, mappers. Keywords: "memory map", "bank switch", "mapper", "mmu", "address space", "rom/ram mapping". Required for systems with complex memory layouts.

Content: GOAL: Create memory system that handles bank switching, mappers, and memory-mapped I/O. Success means: correct address translation, bank switching works, save RAM persistent, memory mirrors handled. Prioritize: flexibility for different mappers, performance for common access patterns.
Content: Memory system patterns across platforms:
**Sprite Multiplexing:** Flicker to show >8-10 sprites per scanline
Universal ROM format design and conversion
CPU core implementation (6502, Z80, 68000, ARM, MIPS, PowerPC, x86)
New system core: S.01 → S.02 → S.03 → S.04 (CPU → Graphics → Audio → Input)

Content: Activate when searching for federal grant opportunities. Keywords: "federal grants", "grants.gov", "usda grants", "epa grants", "federal funding", "government grants". Primary source for all federal opportunities.

Content: GOAL: Identify all relevant federal grant opportunities for Idaho applicant. Success means: current opportunities found, eligibility verified, deadlines noted, application requirements clear. Prioritize: active opportunities first, Idaho-specific programs, realistic timeline for application.

Content: Federal grants are most competitive but largest amounts. Typical timeline: 60-120 days from announcement to deadline. Highly structured applications with strict requirements. SAM.gov registration takes 2-4 weeks - start immediately. Past awards are public - research similar projects. Federal grants require compliance expertise - reporting, audits, procurement rules. Worth effort for large, multi-year funding. Smaller organizations may need technical assistance.
Content: Standard federal grant search workflow:
Keywords: [agriculture / education / health / community development / etc.]
Geographic: Idaho, Region 10 (ID, WA, OR, AK), or nationwide
Content: Activate for rural Idaho projects. Keywords: "usda", "rural development", "rural grants", "agriculture", "rural business", "rural community". Idaho is 95% rural - prime USDA territory.
